entries.title: Dashboard
entries.overview: The Dashboard is your command centre. Every time you log in, start here to see how your business is performing today — sales, stock health, expenses, and activity across all your branches.

before_you_start.text: You must be logged in to your business.
If you have multiple branches, use the Branch filter to focus on one branch or view all.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Select a specific branch to filter all dashboard data to that branch only. Choose 'All Branches' to see the combined view.
elements.notes: Changing this filter reloads all KPI cards and charts automatically.

elements.name: Refresh button
elements.description: Reloads all dashboard data from the server to show the latest numbers. Use this if you've just completed a sale or made changes and want to see updated figures.

elements.name: Sales Today card
elements.description: Shows the total gross sales amount for today in TZS. The mini chart below the number shows the sales trend over the last 8 data points.

elements.name: Revenue MTD card
elements.description: Total net revenue from the 1st of this month to today — gross sales minus refunds and credit notes. Compare against your monthly target to gauge trading performance. A detailed daily breakdown is shown in the Revenue / Sales Trend chart below.

elements.name: Expenses MTD card
elements.description: Total expenses recorded via the Expenses module from the 1st of this month to today. Compare against Revenue MTD for a rough profitability check — a detailed category breakdown is in the Expense Breakdown panel below.

elements.name: Stock Value card
elements.description: Shows the total monetary value of all stock currently in your inventory, and how many product variants are being tracked.

elements.name: Low Stock card
elements.description: Shows how many product variants are below the stock alert threshold (5 units). The sub-label shows how many of those are critically low (2 units or below).

elements.name: Active Branches card
elements.description: Shows how many branches recorded at least one sale this month. Sub-label shows transfers made today.

elements.name: Revenue / Sales Trend chart
elements.description: A line chart showing daily sales totals for the last 30 days. Useful for spotting patterns, slow days, or growth.

elements.name: Top Products panel
elements.description: Shows the 5 products that generated the most revenue this week. The bar next to each product shows its revenue relative to the top performer.

elements.name: Low Stock Alerts panel
elements.description: Lists up to 5 products that are running low on stock. Items highlighted in red are critical (2 units or below). Go to Stock to replenish.

elements.name: Sales by Branch panel
elements.description: Shows how much revenue each branch has generated this month, ranked with a bar chart. Useful if you manage multiple locations.

elements.name: Expense Breakdown panel
elements.description: Shows your top expense categories for this month with amounts and percentage of total spend.

elements.name: Recent Activity panel
elements.description: Shows the last 8 business events — sales, transfers, and alerts — with timestamps. Gives you a quick view of what has happened today.

elements.name: Global Search box
elements.description: Type to search across products, customers, receipts, and transfers. Suggestions appear automatically after 2 characters. Press Enter or click Search to run the full search.

elements.name: Search button
elements.description: Runs the search for whatever is typed in the search box and shows results below in two columns: Products, and Other Matches (receipts, customers, transfers).

elements.name: Open Full Search link
elements.description: Takes you to the dedicated Search page where you can run more detailed searches with additional filters.

common_tasks.task: Check today's sales performance
steps: Look at the Sales Today card for the total.
Check the Revenue / Sales Trend chart to see if today's figure is typical.
If you manage multiple branches, use the Branch filter to check each one.

common_tasks.task: Find a low-stock product
steps: Check the Low Stock card to see how many items need attention.
Look at the Low Stock Alerts panel to see which specific products are low.
Go to Stock > Products to take action.

common_tasks.task: Search for a product or customer
steps: Type in the Global Search box — suggestions appear after 2 characters.
Click a suggestion or press Enter to search.
For more options, click 'Open Full Search'.

warnings: Dashboard data does not update automatically while you are on the page — click Refresh to see the latest figures.
The Low Stock threshold is set at 5 units. Items at 2 or below are flagged as critical.
The Sales Today card shows gross sales (before any expenses or returns). For profit figures, look at the Revenue MTD and Expenses MTD cards together.

related_pages.reason: You must be logged in before accessing the Dashboard.
Use Search for detailed lookups across products, customers, and receipts.
For deeper analysis beyond the dashboard summaries.
Act on low-stock alerts by going to Stock.
View the full list of system notifications and alerts.

entries.title: Approvals
entries.overview: Review and act on pending approval requests. Approval requests are created automatically whenever a transaction exceeds a configured threshold — such as a discount above the limit, a large purchase, a stock adjustment, a refund, or a transfer. This page is the single queue where managers and authorised staff process those requests. Approving a request lets the original transaction complete; rejecting it blocks it permanently and records your reason.

elements.name: Loaded items KPI
elements.description: Total number of approval records fetched in the current view. If the count is high, use the Status or Action filters to narrow down what needs immediate attention.

elements.name: Pending now KPI
elements.description: Count of requests still awaiting a decision. Monitor this regularly — expired requests cannot be reactivated, and the original transaction remains blocked until you act.

elements.name: Status filter KPI
elements.description: Shows which status filter is currently active. PENDING shows only unapproved requests; switch to APPROVED or REJECTED to audit past decisions; EXPIRED shows timed-out requests that were never actioned.

elements.name: Action filter KPI
elements.description: Shows which action type filter is active. Use this to focus on a specific category — for example, filter to DISCOUNT to review all pending discount approvals before the end of a shift.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Free-text search across request data. Useful for finding approvals linked to a specific receipt number, supplier reference, or product name.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, or EXPIRED. EXPIRED means the approval window timed out before anyone acted — the original transaction was automatically blocked and cannot be recovered from this page.

elements.name: Action type filter
elements.description: Filter by the type of action that triggered the request: STOCK_ADJUSTMENT, STOCK_COUNT, REFUND, DISCOUNT, TRANSFER, or PURCHASE_ORDER. Use this when you need to review all requests of one category at once.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restricts results to requests created within the selected date window. Useful for end-of-month reviews or when investigating a specific period.

elements.name: Approve button
elements.description: Immediately approves the request. The system notifies the original requester and the blocked transaction is allowed to complete. This action is final and cannot be undone — review the request detail carefully before clicking.

elements.name: Reject button
elements.description: Opens a prompt where you must enter a rejection reason. Once confirmed, the original transaction is permanently blocked and the requester is notified with your reason. The reason is stored in the audit log.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card view (easier to read individual request details) and table view (better for scanning a large queue at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of approval records. Combine with filters to work through the queue efficiently — for example, filter to PENDING then page through to action everything.

before_you_start.text: You need the approvals.write permission to approve or reject requests. Without it, the Approve and Reject buttons are visible but disabled.
Approval thresholds (the limits that trigger a request) are configured in Business Settings. If you are unsure why a request was generated, check the threshold settings there.

common_tasks.task: Approve a pending request
steps: Set the Status filter to PENDING to see only unapproved requests.
Find the request — use Search or the Action filter to locate it faster if the queue is long.
Click Approve. The requester is notified automatically and the transaction proceeds.

common_tasks.task: Reject a request with a reason
steps: Set the Status filter to PENDING and locate the request.
Click Reject — a prompt appears asking for a rejection reason.
Enter your reason and confirm. The transaction is blocked and the requester is notified with your reason.

common_tasks.task: Review all approvals for a specific action type
steps: Use the Action type filter to select the category (e.g. DISCOUNT or REFUND).
Optionally add a date range to narrow the period.
Use the Status filter to separate PENDING items from historical decisions.

warnings: Expired requests cannot be reactivated. If a request expires, the original user must retry the transaction — a new approval request will be generated.
Approvals are final. Once you click Approve, the action proceeds immediately with no undo. Double-check the request detail before approving large adjustments or refunds.
Only users with the approvals.write permission can approve or reject. If the buttons are disabled, your role may be branch-scoped and the request belongs to a different branch.

entries.title: Attachments
entries.overview: Upload and manage documents linked to purchases or purchase orders — such as supplier invoices, delivery notes, signed contracts, or proof of payment. Each document you select shows its own attachment list. Files are stored securely and can be retrieved at any time for auditing, dispute resolution, or record-keeping.

elements.name: Target type selector
elements.description: Choose whether to search for a Purchase or a Purchase Order. This controls which records appear in the Target document selector below.

elements.name: Target document selector
elements.description: SmartSelect showing all records of the chosen type. Each option includes the supplier name, reference number, and date to help you identify the right document. The attachment list only loads after you select a target document.

elements.name: File input
elements.description: Opens your device's file browser. Select the document you want to upload. File size limits are set by your subscription plan — check the Business Settings subscription panel for your current limits.

elements.name: Upload button
elements.description: Uploads the selected file and links it permanently to the chosen purchase or purchase order. A new attachment entry appears in the list immediately after upload completes.

elements.name: Attachment list
elements.description: Shows all files linked to the selected document. Columns include filename, file size, upload version, and status (ACTIVE or REMOVED). Click a filename to download the file.

elements.name: Remove button
elements.description: Marks an attachment as REMOVED. The file is no longer shown by default but is retained in the audit log for compliance. This cannot be undone from the UI — contact support if you need to restore a removed file.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of attachment records when there are more files than the initial batch.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout and table layout for the attachment list. Table view is better when reviewing many attachments at once.

elements.name: Active count KPI
elements.description: Number of non-removed attachments currently loaded for the selected document type. Use this to confirm an upload succeeded or to spot documents that still need files attached.

elements.name: Total size KPI
elements.description: Combined file size in MB of all loaded attachments. Monitor this against your subscription storage limit.

before_you_start.text: You need the attachments.write permission to upload or remove files.
At least one purchase or purchase order must exist before you can attach files. Create a purchase or PO first from the Purchases page.
You must select a target document before the attachment list loads or the Upload button becomes active.

common_tasks.task: Upload an invoice to a purchase
steps: Set Target type to Purchase.
Select the purchase from the Target document selector (search by supplier or reference).
Click the file input and select the invoice file from your device.
Click Upload. The file appears immediately in the attachment list below.

common_tasks.task: View and download all documents on a purchase order
steps: Set Target type to Purchase Order.
Select the purchase order from the Target document selector.
The attachment list loads. Click any filename to download it to your device.

warnings: Removing an attachment is permanent from a user perspective — there is no restore button in the UI. Only support can recover a removed file.
The Upload button is inactive until you have selected a target document. If it appears greyed out, select a document from the Target document selector first.

entries.title: Audit Logs
entries.overview: An immutable, cryptographically chained log of every action taken in the system — by users, by automated jobs, and by background processes. Every write operation (create, update, delete) generates a log entry capturing who did it, from which device, with what outcome, and what changed (before/after snapshot). Use audit logs to investigate disputes, verify compliance, trace a specific change, or understand the sequence of events during an incident. Logs cannot be modified or deleted — any gap in the hash chain indicates a potential integrity issue.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts results to log entries generated by users operating within a specific branch. Useful for branch managers who only need to review their own branch's activity.

elements.name: User filter
elements.description: Shows only log entries generated by a specific user. Use this when investigating a particular staff member's recent actions — combine with a date range to narrow to a relevant time window.

elements.name: Role filter
elements.description: Filters by the role the user held at the moment the action was taken. Because role assignments can change over time, this reflects the historical role at the time of the event — not the user's current role.

elements.name: Action filter
elements.description: Filters by the type of database operation: CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, READ, LOGIN, LOGOUT, EXPORT, and others. For example, filter to DELETE to see all records that were removed.

elements.name: Outcome filter
elements.description: Filters by the result of the action: SUCCESS or FAILURE. Filtering to FAILURE is useful for identifying permission denials, validation errors, or repeated failed login attempts.

elements.name: Resource Type filter
elements.description: Narrows results to a specific entity type, such as Sale, Transfer, User, Product, or Approval. Combine with Resource ID to find all log entries for one specific record.

elements.name: Resource ID filter
elements.description: Finds all log entries related to a single record, identified by its ID. Enter or paste the ID directly, or use the typeahead search to look it up by name. Requires Resource Type to be selected first.

elements.name: Advanced filters toggle
elements.description: Reveals a second row of technical filters: correlation ID (all logs from one HTTP request chain), request ID (one specific request), session ID (all actions in one login session), device ID (all actions from a registered offline device), offline flag (actions taken while offline), and approval status. Use these when tracing a complex incident.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Limits results to a specific time window. The system stores logs indefinitely — always apply a date range when searching to avoid loading unnecessarily large result sets.

elements.name: Activity filter buttons
elements.description: Toggle switches to suppress noisy, routine log entries: guard permission checks, dashboard data refreshes, and background auth token renewals. Hiding these reveals the meaningful user-initiated actions more clearly.

elements.name: Log card / row
elements.description: Summarises a single log entry: a human-readable action narrative (e.g. 'User Jane updated Product SKU-042'), an outcome badge (SUCCESS or FAILURE), timestamp, branch, user, and resource type/ID. Click or expand to see the full before/after snapshot, metadata, and hash.

elements.name: Diff highlights
elements.description: Visible inside an expanded log entry for UPDATE actions. Shows each field that changed with the old value (from) and new value (to) side by side. Fields that were not changed are omitted to keep the diff concise.

elements.name: View Chain button
elements.description: Loads all log entries that share the same correlation ID as this entry — i.e., all actions triggered by a single HTTP request chain. For example, approving a sale may trigger a stock movement, a receipt, and a notification — View Chain shows all of those together in sequence.

elements.name: Related links
elements.description: Contextual deep links to the relevant module page for the log's resource type. For example, a log entry for a Sale includes a link to the Receipts page filtered to that receipt. Use these to jump from the audit trail directly to the affected record.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (expanded context per entry, good for investigation) and table layout (denser, more entries visible, good for scanning a timeline).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered pagination to navigate through large result sets. Results load 50 entries per page.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'audit.read' permission to access this page.
Audit logs are read-only and cannot be modified, deleted, or exported from this view.
Apply a date range before searching — the system stores logs indefinitely and unbounded queries can be slow.

common_tasks.task: Find all actions taken by a specific user
steps: Open the User filter and select the user.
Set a date range to the relevant period.
Review the results — use the Action filter to narrow to specific operation types if needed.
Expand any row to see the full before/after diff.

common_tasks.task: Investigate what changed on a specific record
steps: Select the Resource Type for the record (e.g. Product).
Enter or search for the Resource ID.
Review the log entries — each UPDATE will show what changed via Diff highlights.
Use View Chain to see any linked actions that were triggered by the same operation.

common_tasks.task: Trace the full sequence of an operation
steps: Find the primary log entry for the action.
Click 'View Chain' to load all correlated log entries.
Review the full sequence in chronological order to understand every step that was triggered.

warnings: Audit logs are append-only and cannot be edited or deleted by anyone — this is by design for compliance.
Each log entry is hash-chained to the previous one. A gap or mismatch in the chain is a serious integrity signal.
The Resource ID filter requires the Resource Type to be selected first — searches without a type may return no results.

entries.title: Categories
entries.overview: Manage your product taxonomy. Categories organize products into groups, support nested hierarchies (parent/child), and are required before creating products.

elements.name: KPI – Categories
elements.description: Total categories loaded in the current filter scope. Without filters, this is your complete taxonomy size. Every product must belong to a category, so this number should reflect your intended product structure.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE categories. Only ACTIVE categories appear in the Category Selector when creating products. INACTIVE categories are hidden from those flows — if a category is missing during product setup, check here first.

elements.name: KPI – Parent Groups
elements.description: Count of top-level categories that have no parent assigned. These are your root taxonomy groups (e.g. 'Beverages', 'Snacks'). If this number is lower than expected, some categories may be misconfigured with a parent when they should be top-level.

elements.name: KPI – Filtered Status
elements.description: Reflects the current Status filter so you can tell at a glance which category set you are browsing — for example 'ACTIVE' confirms the list shows only live categories available for product assignment.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Find categories by typing part of the name. Results update automatically as you type. Useful when navigating a large taxonomy to locate a specific group before editing or assigning products to it.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Narrows results by category status: ACTIVE (visible and usable in product creation), INACTIVE (disabled — products cannot be assigned to it), or ARCHIVED (soft-deleted, hidden from all flows). Use INACTIVE to find categories temporarily suspended.

elements.name: Category Name Input
elements.description: The display name for the new category. This name appears in the Category Selector on the Products page, in report filters, and in POS navigation. Choose names that are clear and consistent with your existing taxonomy — avoid abbreviations that other staff won't recognise.

elements.name: Parent Selector
elements.description: Optional. Assigns this category under an existing parent. Leave empty to create a top-level group.

elements.name: Create Category Button
elements.description: Creates the category and adds it to the list immediately. If the Parent selector is empty, it becomes a top-level group. The new category is immediately available as a product category assignment. Requires catalog.write permission.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid and table layout for the categories list.

elements.name: Edit Button (per category)
elements.description: Opens the category's name and parent fields for inline editing on the same card or row. Make your changes then click Save to commit, or Cancel to discard without affecting the category. Requires catalog.write permission.

elements.name: Save / Cancel (inline edit)
elements.description: Saves or discards the edit. The parent selector excludes the category itself to prevent self-parent loops.

elements.name: Load More Button
elements.description: Loads the next page of categories when more results exist beyond the current page.

before_you_start.text: catalog.read is required to list categories; catalog.write is required to create or edit them.
Plan your top-level groups first, then child categories — hierarchy is hard to restructure later.
Search for an existing category before creating a new one to avoid duplicates.

common_tasks.task: Create a new top-level category
steps: Enter the category name.
Leave the Parent selector empty.
Click Create Category and confirm the success banner.

common_tasks.task: Move a category under a different parent
steps: Find the category in cards or table view.
Click Edit and select the new parent.
Click Save and verify the parent label updates.

warnings: Renaming a category affects how products appear in filters and reports — confirm with your team before changing names.
The parent selector blocks self-parent assignment, but circular hierarchies can still be created through multiple edits.
Categories cannot be deleted from this page — archive them instead.

related_pages.reason: Products require a category before they can be created.
Variants inherit category context through their parent products.

entries.title: Products
entries.overview: Create and manage product records. Products are the parent container for variants. Each product belongs to a category and can have multiple images. Use the Product Wizard for guided full setup, or the quick form here for basic creation.

elements.name: KPI – Products
elements.description: Total products matching current filters. Without any filters, this is your full catalogue size. Products without variants cannot be sold — a product with zero variants will not appear in the POS.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE products in the current view. Only ACTIVE products are visible in POS search and sales flows. If a product is missing from the POS, check here to confirm its status.

elements.name: KPI – With Images
elements.description: Count of products that have at least one active image. Products without images display a placeholder in the POS and customer-facing views. Use this to identify which products still need photos uploaded.

elements.name: KPI – Catalog Focus
elements.description: Shows which category filter is active — or 'All Categories' when none is selected. Helps you orient yourself when browsing a large catalogue. If you need to work within a specific category, set the Category Filter below to lock to it.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Find products by name. Results update 350ms after you stop typing. Use this to quickly navigate a large catalogue to a specific product for editing, image upload, or to open the View Related panel and check stock.

elements.name: Category Filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to products in a specific category. Use this when you need to review or update all products in one section of your catalogue — for example, checking all 'Beverages' have images before a promotion.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by product status: ACTIVE (visible in POS and sales), INACTIVE (hidden from POS), or ARCHIVED (soft-deleted, removed from all live flows). Filter to INACTIVE to find products accidentally deactivated.

elements.name: Has Variants Filter
elements.description: Filter to products that do or do not have at least one variant. Products with no variants cannot appear in the POS — use 'No Variants' to find products that still need variants created before they can be sold.

elements.name: Has Images Filter
elements.description: Filter to products that do or do not have at least one active image. Use 'No Images' to create a work list of products that still need photos — unimaged products appear as placeholders in the POS and customer-facing views.

elements.name: Open Wizard Button
elements.description: Navigates to the Product Wizard for guided product + variant creation in a single flow.

elements.name: Product Name Input
elements.description: The display name as it will appear on receipts, in reports, and in POS search results. Make it specific enough to distinguish from similar products (e.g. 'Coca-Cola 500ml' rather than just 'Coke').

elements.name: Description Input
elements.description: Optional text description visible in product detail views — not printed on receipts. Use it for internal notes such as supplier codes, storage instructions, or key specifications.

elements.name: Category Selector
elements.description: Required. Assigns the product to a category, which determines how it appears in POS category navigation and in reports. A product cannot be saved without selecting a category. Create the category first if it does not exist.

elements.name: Create Product Button
elements.description: Creates the product record and adds it to the list. The product has no variants yet — after creation, go to the Variants page or use the Product Wizard to add SKUs with pricing, barcodes, and stock.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid (with images and related panels) and compact table view.

elements.name: View Related Button (per product)
elements.description: Expands a panel showing the product's variants, stock on hand (in active branch), and recent stock movements.

elements.name: Primary Image Upload (per product)
elements.description: Uploads and sets the product's main display image (PNG or JPG, max 20 MB). This image appears in the POS product grid and on receipts if image printing is enabled. The first image uploaded automatically becomes the primary.

elements.name: Additional Image Upload (per product)
elements.description: Uploads extra images — only available if your subscription allows multiple images per product.

elements.name: Make Primary Button (per image)
elements.description: Promotes an existing non-primary image to become the product's main display image. The previously primary image is demoted to a secondary image. Use this to swap the display image without deleting the current one.

elements.name: Remove Button (per image)
elements.description: Deactivates the selected image — it is no longer served as the product photo but is not permanently deleted. If you remove the primary image, the next remaining image is automatically promoted to primary.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate pages and adjust page size for the product list.

before_you_start.text: At least one category must exist — create categories first at /catalog/categories.
catalog.read is required to list products; catalog.write is required to create products and manage images.
Use the Product Wizard (/catalog/products/wizard) when setting up a new product with variants and pricing in one flow.

common_tasks.task: Create a simple product
steps: Enter the product name.
Select a category.
Click Create Product and confirm the success banner.

common_tasks.task: Upload a product image
steps: Switch to Cards view.
Find the product card.
Click the Primary Image file input and select a PNG or JPG under 20 MB.
The image appears on the card once uploaded.

warnings: Category is required — you cannot create a product without selecting one first.
The first image uploaded must be the primary image; additional images require a subscription that allows multiple images.
View Related loads live stock data — if the branch scope is wrong, the numbers shown will be misleading.
Product images are served publicly — do not upload sensitive or confidential material.

related_pages.reason: Categories must exist before products can be created.
Variants are created after the product exists.
The wizard guides full product + variant creation in one flow.

entries.title: New Product Wizard
entries.overview: A 4-step guided form to create a new product with one or more variants and set opening stock quantities. A product is the parent item (e.g. 'Bottled Water'); variants are the sellable versions (e.g. '500ml', '1L'). Each variant gets its own SKU, price, cost, VAT setting, and optional barcode. You can set initial stock quantities per variant per branch on step 3.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your position across the four steps: Product → Variants → Initial Stock → Review. Each step must be completed before advancing. Click Back at any step to return and make changes before submitting.

elements.name: Product name input
elements.description: Required. The product's display name — shown in POS product search, inventory reports, transfer forms, and the catalogue. Use a name that your staff will recognise and search for quickly.

elements.name: Description input
elements.description: Optional. A longer description of the product — for example materials, intended use, or key features. Displayed on product detail pages and in some report exports.

elements.name: Category selector
elements.description: Assign the product to a category. Categories group products in the POS product browser, catalogue filters, and reports. Categories must exist before using this wizard — create them from the main Catalog page.

elements.name: Variant row
elements.description: One row per variant. A variant is a distinct, sellable version of the product — for example 'Small', '500ml', or 'Blue'. Each variant has its own name, SKU (stock keeping unit — must be unique in your catalogue), default sale price, minimum price (the floor below which discounts cannot go), default cost (used for margin calculations), VAT mode, stock tracking toggle, and optional barcode.

elements.name: VAT mode selector (per variant)
elements.description: INCLUSIVE — the sale price already includes VAT; the system extracts the VAT portion at sale time. EXCLUSIVE — VAT is added on top of the price at the point of sale. EXEMPT — no VAT applies to this variant. Choose according to the local tax treatment for this specific product.

elements.name: Track stock toggle (per variant)
elements.description: When enabled, every sale, receipt, transfer, and adjustment for this variant creates a stock movement record and affects the physical stock count. Disable only for service items or non-physical products you do not want to track — for example a delivery fee line item.

elements.name: Barcode input + Scan button
elements.description: Enter a barcode number manually or click Scan to use the device camera to read it. The barcode is used at POS for barcode scanner lookup — scanning a product at the till finds its variant automatically. Each barcode must be unique across your entire catalogue.

elements.name: Base unit / Sell unit selectors
elements.description: Base unit is how you receive and track stock (e.g. Case). Sell unit is how you sell it at POS (e.g. Bottle). The conversion factor tells the system how many sell units make one base unit (e.g. 24 bottles per case — enter 24). If you buy and sell in the same unit, set both to the same unit and set conversion factor to 1. Units must be configured in Business Settings before they appear here.

elements.name: Add Variant button
elements.description: Adds another variant row to the product. Use this for products that come in multiple sizes, colours, flavours, or packaging options — each becomes a separately tracked SKU in your catalogue with its own stock level.

elements.name: Remove Variant button
elements.description: Removes that variant row before the product is created. At least one variant is required — you cannot remove the last row.

elements.name: Stock lines (Initial Stock step)
elements.description: One row per variant per branch. Enter the opening stock quantity for each variant at each branch. This creates an opening stock movement record that sets the starting inventory level. If you are onboarding an existing product, enter the quantity currently on hand at each branch.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary of all data entered: product name, category, each variant's name, SKU, prices, and cost, plus initial stock quantities per branch. Review every field carefully — clicking Submit creates all records permanently. There is no bulk undo after submission.

elements.name: Next / Back buttons
elements.description: Move forward or backward between wizard steps. Back does not discard your current step's data.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Creates the product, all variants, and the initial stock movement records. The product is immediately available in POS product search, receiving selectors, transfer forms, and the catalogue. Requires the catalog.write permission.

before_you_start.text: You need the catalog.write permission.
At least one category must exist before using this wizard. Create categories from the main Catalog page.
Units (base and sell) must be configured in Business Settings before they appear in the unit selectors.
Barcode scanning requires camera permission to be granted in your browser.

common_tasks.task: Add a new product with one variant
steps: On the Product step, enter the product name and select a category. Click Next.
On the Variants step, the first row is pre-filled — enter the SKU, default price, minimum price, and default cost. Set the VAT mode and enable Track stock. Add a barcode if applicable. Click Next.
On the Initial Stock step, enter the opening quantity for each branch. Click Next.
On the Review step, verify all details. Click Submit to create the product.

common_tasks.task: Add a product with multiple variants (e.g. sizes)
steps: On the Variants step, fill in the first variant row (e.g. 'Small / SKU-001').
Click Add Variant to add a second row (e.g. 'Large / SKU-002') and fill in its details.
Repeat for as many variants as needed — each must have a unique SKU.
Continue to Initial Stock and enter quantities for each variant at each branch.

common_tasks.task: Set up a product with different base and sell units (e.g. case and bottle)
steps: On the Variants step, set Base unit to Case and Sell unit to Bottle.
Set the conversion factor to the number of bottles per case (e.g. 24).
Stock is tracked in Cases — when a Bottle is sold, the system deducts 1/24 of a Case from inventory.

warnings: SKUs must be unique across your entire catalogue. Duplicate SKUs are rejected on submission — each variant must have a distinct SKU before you can proceed.

Initial stock entered here creates opening stock movement records. If you enter the wrong quantity, correct it with a manual stock adjustment later — there is no way to edit initial stock after submission.
Conversion factor must be a positive number greater than zero. Set it to 1 if base and sell units are the same. An incorrect conversion factor causes quantity errors in stock tracking and reports.

entries.title: Variants
entries.overview: Manage SKU-level records for your products. Each variant holds its own pricing, unit configuration, barcodes, branch availability, and image. This page also handles barcode scanning, reassignment, and label printing.

elements.name: KPI – Variants
elements.description: Total variants in scope based on current filters. This is the count available for label printing when using Select All. If the number does not match your expected catalogue, check the Status or Branch filter.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE variants in the current page. Only ACTIVE variants appear in POS search and stock flows. If a variant is missing from the POS, filter here to ACTIVE to confirm it exists and is enabled.

elements.name: KPI – With Barcode
elements.description: Count of variants with at least one active barcode assigned. Variants without barcodes cannot be found by scanning in the POS — use this number to identify how many variants still need barcodes added or printed.

elements.name: KPI – Branches
elements.description: Number of branches loaded for branch availability management. Each variant can be marked available or unavailable per branch — this tells you how many branches the current availability context covers.

elements.name: Product Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Required. The parent product this variant belongs to. Only ACTIVE and INACTIVE products appear. Once a variant is saved, its parent product cannot be changed — if you select the wrong product, you will need to create a new variant.

elements.name: Variant Name Input
elements.description: Required display name for the variant as it appears on receipts and in the POS (e.g. '1 Litre', 'Red – Large', '500ml'). Be specific enough that cashiers can distinguish between variants of the same product at a glance.

elements.name: SKU Input
elements.description: Optional internal stock-keeping unit code used for purchase orders, imports, and inventory tracking. If left blank the system will generate an identifier. Reassigning an SKU later requires a written reason and may trigger an approval workflow.

elements.name: Barcode Input
elements.description: Optional barcode to assign at creation time. Can also be filled by scanning with the Scan Assign New button. Barcodes enable quick product lookup at the POS — variants without barcodes must be found by name search.

elements.name: Scan Assign New Button
elements.description: Activates the device camera to scan a barcode and automatically fills the Barcode Input field with the scanned code. Use this instead of typing the barcode manually to avoid transcription errors.

elements.name: Default Price Input
elements.description: The standard selling price for this variant in TZS. This is what the POS suggests by default when this variant is added to a sale. If a price list applies to the customer, the price list price overrides this default at the point of sale.

elements.name: Min Price Input
elements.description: Floor price for this variant in TZS. If a cashier tries to sell below this price, the POS displays a warning and may require manager approval depending on your business's pricing rule configuration.

elements.name: VAT Mode Selector
elements.description: Controls how VAT is applied to this variant's price: INCLUSIVE (VAT is included in the displayed price), EXCLUSIVE (VAT is added on top at checkout), or EXEMPT (no VAT applies). This setting affects how tax is calculated on receipts and in financial reports.

elements.name: Base Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit in which stock quantities are tracked in the system (e.g. Each, Kg, Litre). Stock movements, adjustments, and counts are all recorded in this unit. Choose it to match how you physically count and store the item.

elements.name: Sell Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit in which the variant is sold (e.g. Pack, Box). Can differ from the base unit.

elements.name: Conversion Factor Input
elements.description: How many base units equal one sell unit. Disabled and locked to 1 when both units are the same.

elements.name: Create Variant Button
elements.description: Saves the new variant and adds it to the catalogue. The variant starts with zero stock on hand — make an opening stock adjustment or receive a purchase to add initial stock before selling.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – Barcode Selector
elements.description: Selects the existing barcode to move to a different variant. The dropdown shows the barcode value and the variant it currently belongs to — confirm you have selected the right code before proceeding.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – New Variant Selector
elements.description: The variant that will take ownership of the barcode after reassignment. The barcode is unlinked from its current variant and assigned here. The previous variant will have no barcode until one is manually added.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – Reason Input
elements.description: Required. A written explanation for why this barcode is being moved to a different variant (e.g. 'Old SKU discontinued — barcode reassigned to replacement variant'). Stored in the audit log alongside the reassignment event.

elements.name: Submit Reassignment Button
elements.description: Submits the reassignment. May return 'approval required' if your business policy requires a review.

elements.name: Scan Start / Restart Button
elements.description: Activates the device camera for two purposes: scanning a barcode to look up which variant it belongs to, or scanning to fill the barcode field during variant creation. Click Restart if the scanner freezes or loses focus.

elements.name: Scan Stop Button
elements.description: Stops the active camera scan session and returns the scanner to idle. The last scan result remains visible — use this after completing a lookup or barcode assignment.

elements.name: Scan Result Panel
elements.description: Shows the product, variant name, and SKU found for the most recently scanned barcode.

elements.name: Print A4 Labels Button
elements.description: Generates barcode labels for selected variants and triggers an A4 print dialog.

elements.name: Print Thermal Labels Button
elements.description: Generates barcode label sheets formatted for thermal label printers and opens the print dialog. Labels are sized for standard thermal roll widths. Only selected (checked) variants are included in the print job.

elements.name: Variant Checkbox (per card)
elements.description: Selects this variant for inclusion in the next label print job. Tick the variants you need labels for, then click Print A4 Labels or Print Thermal Labels. Clear all checkboxes between different print batches to avoid printing unwanted labels.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Filter the variant list by name or SKU code. Results update as you type. Use this to quickly find a variant for editing, barcode assignment, or to verify details before printing labels.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by variant status: ACTIVE (available in POS and stock flows), INACTIVE (hidden from POS), or ARCHIVED (soft-deleted, removed from all live flows). If a variant is missing from the POS, filter to INACTIVE to find it.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Narrows the variant list to the availability context of a specific branch. Use this when managing which variants are marked available at a particular location, then use the Availability Filter to refine further.

elements.name: Availability Filter
elements.description: Filter to variants that are marked available (or unavailable) in the currently selected branch. Use 'Unavailable' to find variants that have been blocked from sale at this location and decide whether to re-enable them.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid and table view for the variants list.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate pages and adjust page size.

before_you_start.text: Parent products must exist — create products first at /catalog/products.
Units must be configured in settings before you can set base/sell units on variants.
catalog.write is required for all create, update, barcode, and image operations.

common_tasks.task: Create a new variant
steps: Select the parent product.
Enter the variant name.
Set base unit and sell unit (and conversion factor if they differ).
Optionally enter a price and barcode.
Click Create Variant.

common_tasks.task: Print barcode labels
steps: Tick the checkbox on each variant card you want to label.
Click Print A4 Labels or Print Thermal Labels.
The browser print dialog opens with the generated label sheet.

warnings: Barcode reassignment may require approval — the result panel will indicate if approval is needed before the change takes effect.
SKU reassignment also requires a reason and may trigger an approval workflow.
Conversion factor must be a positive non-zero number when base unit differs from sell unit.
Camera scanning requires the browser to have permission to access the device camera.
Label printing includes all checked variants — clear selections between different print jobs.

related_pages.reason: Products must exist before variants can be created.
View stock levels after setting up variants.

entries.title: Customers
entries.overview: Manage your customer directory. Create and maintain customer profiles, assign price lists so the POS applies their negotiated prices automatically, track outstanding credit balances, and archive or anonymise customers when needed. Customers linked at POS also appear in receipt history, enabling credit settlement and return lookups.

elements.name: KPI — Customers
elements.description: Total number of customer records matching the current filters. If no filters are set, this is your total customer count.

elements.name: KPI — Active
elements.description: Count of customers with ACTIVE status. Only ACTIVE customers appear in POS customer selectors — if a customer is missing at POS, check their status here.

elements.name: KPI — Price list linked
elements.description: Count of customers with a price list assigned. These customers automatically get their custom prices applied at POS when they are selected during a sale.

elements.name: KPI — Current filter
elements.description: Shows which status filter is currently active, or 'All statuses' if no filter is set. Use this as a quick reminder of what you are currently viewing.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter customers by name, phone, or email as you type. Use this to locate a specific customer quickly when your list is large.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrow the list to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED customers. ARCHIVED customers are hidden from POS selectors but their purchase history and credit records are preserved.

elements.name: Balance due filter
elements.description: Filter to customers with an outstanding credit balance (Yes) or a clear balance (No). Use Yes to identify all customers who currently owe money — useful before making collection calls or running credit settlement sessions.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active search and filter inputs and reloads the full customer list. Use this to start a fresh search after narrowing results too far.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (shows all details per customer) and table layout (better for scanning many customers at once).

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. The customer's display name — shown in POS customer selectors, receipts, and reports. Use the name you and your staff will recognise them by.

elements.name: Phone field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The customer's phone number. Useful for contact and for identifying returning customers at POS.

elements.name: Email field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The customer's email address. Stored for reference and for any future email notification features.

elements.name: TIN field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The customer's Tax Identification Number. Required for issuing formal tax invoices to business customers in compliance with local tax regulations.

elements.name: Notes field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Free-text notes about the customer — for example preferred payment method, delivery preferences, or VIP status notes. Visible to all staff who can see the customer record.

elements.name: Default price list (create form)
elements.description: Optionally link the customer to a price list. When this customer is selected at POS, their linked price list is automatically applied — overriding the branch's default prices. Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page.

elements.name: Create Customer button
elements.description: Saves the new customer record. The customer is immediately available in POS customer selectors. Requires the customers.create permission.

elements.name: Edit button (per customer)
elements.description: Opens an inline edit form for that customer's name, phone, email, TIN, notes, price list, and status. Requires the customers.update permission.

elements.name: Archive button (per customer)
elements.description: Sets the customer status to ARCHIVED. The customer is removed from POS selectors but all their purchase history and credit records are preserved. Requires the customers.update permission.

elements.name: Anonymise button (per customer)
elements.description: Permanently and irreversibly removes all personal data (name, phone, email, TIN, notes) from the customer record. The transaction history is preserved but is no longer linked to an identifiable person. Use this to comply with privacy or GDPR deletion requests. Requires the customers.anonymize permission.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits the edited fields immediately. Cancel discards all changes and closes the inline edit form without saving.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Fetches the next page of customers and appends them to the list. Use this if a customer does not appear in the initial results — they may be on a later page or you may need to adjust your filters.

before_you_start.text: You need the customers.create permission to add new customers.
You need the customers.update permission to edit or archive a customer.
You need the customers.anonymize permission to permanently remove a customer's personal data.
Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page before they can be assigned to customers here.
Anonymisation is irreversible — personal data cannot be recovered after this action. Use it only for genuine privacy or legal deletion requests.

common_tasks.task: Add a new customer
steps: Fill in the Name (required) and optionally phone, email, TIN, and notes.
If this customer has negotiated prices, select their price list from Default price list.
Click Create Customer. The customer is immediately available at POS.

common_tasks.task: Assign a price list to a customer
steps: Find the customer card and click Edit.
Select the price list from the Default price list field.
Click Save. The next time this customer is selected at POS, their custom prices apply automatically.

common_tasks.task: Archive a customer
steps: Find the customer card and click Archive.
Confirm the action. The customer is removed from POS selectors but all history and credit records are kept.

common_tasks.task: Anonymise a customer (privacy / GDPR request)
steps: Find the customer card and click Anonymise.
Read the warning carefully — this action is permanent.
Confirm. All personal data is removed immediately. Transaction history is retained but is no longer personally identifiable.

common_tasks.task: Find customers with outstanding balances
steps: Set Balance due filter to Yes.
The list shows only customers who currently owe money.
Use this list to run credit settlement sessions or make collection calls.

warnings: Anonymisation is permanent and cannot be undone. Once personal data is removed, it cannot be recovered — confirm with your manager before using this feature.
Archiving a customer hides them from POS but does not delete their purchase history or credit balance. The credit balance is still visible here for settlement.

Assigning a price list to a customer overrides branch-level pricing at POS. If the customer's prices seem wrong, check both the customer's price list assignment here and the branch's price list assignment on the Branch Settings page.

entries.title: Expenses
entries.overview: Record and track business expenses that are not goods purchases — such as rent, utilities, payroll, bank transfer fees, and shipping costs. Expenses are categorised so they group correctly in reports. Each expense is linked to a branch for branch-level cost tracking. Unlike purchases, expenses do not affect stock levels.

elements.name: Branch selector (create form)
elements.description: Choose which branch this expense belongs to. Expenses are tracked per branch so they appear correctly in branch-level cost reports. You must have access to the selected branch.

elements.name: Category selector
elements.description: Select the category that best describes the expense. RENT — premises costs; UTILITIES — electricity, water, internet; PAYROLL — staff wages and salaries; SHIPPING — delivery and freight costs; TRANSFER_FEE — bank or mobile money transaction fees; GENERAL — anything that does not fit a specific category; OTHER — use sparingly and add a note. Category is used for grouping in reports — choose consistently for meaningful expense analysis.

elements.name: Amount input
elements.description: The full amount of the expense. Required — cannot be zero. For partial payments or installments, record each payment as a separate expense entry.

elements.name: Currency input
elements.description: The currency the expense was paid in. Defaults to your business currency. If you pay expenses in a foreign currency, enter the amount as-paid — the system stores it without converting, so use your reporting tools to handle multi-currency reconciliation.

elements.name: Note input
elements.description: Optional free-text description of what the expense was for. Be specific — for example 'April rent for Main Street branch' or 'Electricity bill — ref INV-2024-04'. This note appears in the expense list and in exports.

elements.name: Receipt reference input
elements.description: Optional reference number from a physical receipt or invoice. Use this to link the system record to a paper document — for example the invoice number from your landlord or utility provider. Helps with audits and reconciliation.

elements.name: Expense date picker
elements.description: The date the expense was incurred (not necessarily today). Defaults to today. Change this if you are recording a past expense — the date determines which reporting period the expense falls into.

elements.name: Save Expense button
elements.description: Creates the expense record immediately. Expenses cannot be edited after saving — verify the amount, date, and category before clicking. If a mistake is made, you will need to record a correcting entry.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filter the expense list by keyword. Searches across the note text, receipt reference, and category names.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restrict the expense list to a specific branch. Useful for branch managers reviewing their own operating costs.

elements.name: Category filter
elements.description: Filter the expense list by category — for example, show only RENT or PAYROLL expenses. Use this when totalling a specific cost type for a period.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by expense status. PENDING means the expense is recorded but may be awaiting approval if thresholds are configured. COMPLETED means fully processed. REJECTED means an approval was denied.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restrict results to expenses incurred within the selected date window. Use this for monthly reporting or end-of-period cost reviews.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of expense records. Use filters first to narrow the result set before paging.

before_you_start.text: You need the expenses.write permission to record expenses.
The branch must exist before you can record an expense against it. Create branches from the Branch Settings page.

common_tasks.task: Record a monthly rent payment
steps: Select the branch the rent applies to.
Set Category to RENT and enter the amount and currency.
Set Expense date to the date the rent was paid.
Add the landlord's invoice number in Receipt reference (optional but recommended).
Click Save Expense.

common_tasks.task: View all utility expenses for a branch this month
steps: Set Branch filter to the specific branch.
Set Category filter to UTILITIES.
Set the date range From/To to cover the current month.
The filtered list shows all utility expenses in that period.

common_tasks.task: Record a bank transfer fee
steps: Set Category to TRANSFER_FEE.
Enter the fee amount and the currency it was charged in.
Add a note identifying the related transaction — for example 'Transfer fee for supplier payment to ABC Ltd on 2026-03-01'.
Click Save Expense.

warnings: Expenses cannot be edited after saving. If an amount or date was entered incorrectly, record a correcting entry and flag the error for your accountant — there is no delete function either.
Expenses do not affect stock levels or the purchase ledger. For goods received from suppliers, use the Purchase Orders workflow — not Expenses.
The currency is stored as-entered without conversion. When reconciling foreign-currency expenses in reports, filter by currency and apply your own exchange rate calculation.

entries.title: Exports & Imports
entries.overview: Create bulk data export jobs (CSV or ZIP files) for use in spreadsheets, accounting systems, or external reporting tools. Also supports CSV bulk imports for categories, products, opening stock, price updates, and status changes. Exports run as background jobs and are available for download once complete. Large exports may take a few minutes.

elements.name: Export type selector
elements.description: Choose what data to export. STOCK — current stock levels by variant and branch. PRODUCTS — full product and variant catalogue. OPENING_STOCK — opening stock movement records. PRICE_UPDATES — price change history. SUPPLIERS — full supplier directory. BRANCHES — branch list with details. CUSTOMERS — customer directory. RECEIPTS — sales receipt records. PURCHASES — purchase records. AUDIT_LOGS — full system audit trail (requires acknowledgement checkbox). USERS — user list. ROLES — role and permission definitions.

elements.name: Branch selector (export)
elements.description: Optionally restrict the export to a specific branch. Leave blank to export data across all branches. Stock and receipt exports are especially useful filtered by branch for branch-level reviews.

elements.name: Audit acknowledgement checkbox
elements.description: Required before you can create an AUDIT_LOGS export. By ticking this, you confirm that you understand the compliance implications of exporting a full audit trail, which contains sensitive operational history.

elements.name: Create Export button
elements.description: Queues the export job. The job runs in the background — for large datasets it may take several minutes. Once complete, the job appears in the export history list below with a Download button.

elements.name: Import type selector
elements.description: Choose what type of data to import. Categories — bulk-create product categories. Products — bulk-create products and variants. Opening stock — set initial stock quantities for existing variants. Price updates — bulk-update variant prices. Status updates — change the status of existing records in bulk.

elements.name: CSV textarea (import)
elements.description: Paste your CSV content here. The CSV must match the expected format for the chosen import type — download a template or check the documentation for the required column headers and format. Incorrect headers or formatting will cause validation errors.

elements.name: Preview button
elements.description: Validates your CSV content and shows a preview before importing. Displays the number of valid rows, invalid rows, and detailed error messages with row numbers for any problems. Fix all errors before clicking Apply.

elements.name: Preview table
elements.description: Shows the first rows of valid data and a list of errors with row numbers. Read each error carefully — common issues include missing required columns, duplicate SKUs, or unrecognised category names. Fix the CSV and re-paste it before previewing again.

elements.name: Apply button
elements.description: Imports all validated rows from the CSV. This is irreversible — records are created or updated immediately. Only click Apply after the Preview shows zero errors and the data looks correct.

elements.name: Search input (job history)
elements.description: Filter the export job history by keyword, matching against the export name, type, and description. Useful when looking for a specific export you ran previously among many job records.

elements.name: Status filter (job history)
elements.description: Filter export jobs by status. PENDING — queued but not yet running. RUNNING — currently being processed. COMPLETED — finished and ready to download. FAILED — encountered an error; check the worker status panel and retry.

elements.name: Type filter (job history)
elements.description: Narrows the job list to a specific export type (e.g. STOCK, SALES, CUSTOMERS, AUDIT). Use this when you need to find all exports of one kind — for example, every stock snapshot exported this quarter.

elements.name: Branch filter (job list)
elements.description: Narrows the job list to exports that were scoped to a specific branch. Use this when reviewing export history for a particular location or confirming that a branch-level report was generated.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Limits the job history to exports created within the selected date window. Set this to find exports from a specific reporting period without scrolling through the full history.

elements.name: Download button (per job)
elements.description: Downloads the completed export file (CSV or ZIP) for that job. Only available once the job status is COMPLETED.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (more detail per job including status badge, file size, and download link) and table layout (more compact, better for scanning many jobs at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered page navigation for the export job history list. Each page loads 25 job records.

elements.name: Worker status panel
elements.description: Shows the current state of the export processing queue: how many jobs are pending, how many are running, and how many have failed. If jobs are stuck in PENDING for a long time, or if FAILED count is rising, the export worker may need to be restarted — contact support.

before_you_start.text: You need the exports.manage permission to create exports or run imports.
Large exports run asynchronously — the job appears in the history list below and can take several minutes before the Download button becomes available.
For imports, prepare your CSV carefully and match the exact column headers for the import type. Use the Preview step to catch errors before committing.

common_tasks.task: Export a stock snapshot
steps: Set Export type to STOCK.
Optionally select a branch to restrict the export to one location.
Click Create Export. The job appears in the history list below.
Wait for the status to change to COMPLETED, then click Download.

common_tasks.task: Import products from a CSV file
steps: Set Import type to Products.
Paste your prepared CSV into the CSV textarea.
Click Preview. Review the validation results — fix any errors in your CSV and re-paste if needed.
When Preview shows zero errors, click Apply to import all valid rows.

common_tasks.task: Download a previous export
steps: In the export job history, set Status filter to COMPLETED.
Find the export you want using the Type filter or Date range.
Click Download to save the CSV or ZIP file.

warnings: Apply (import) is irreversible — imported records are created or updated immediately. Preview carefully and ensure zero errors before clicking Apply.
AUDIT_LOGS export requires the acknowledgement checkbox to be ticked — this is a compliance safeguard. The exported file contains sensitive operational history.
If export jobs are stuck in PENDING status or the Failed count is rising in the worker status panel, the export processing worker may be down. Contact support to investigate.

entries.title: Accept Invitation
entries.overview: Use this page if you have been invited to join a business by an owner or manager. The invitation link sent to your email will take you here automatically with the token pre-filled. Enter your name and set a password to create your account.

before_you_start.text: You must have received an invitation email from a business owner or manager.
If you are registering a new business (not joining one), use the Sign Up page instead.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The invitation token from your email. If you arrived via the invitation link, this is pre-filled and cannot be edited. If you are entering the token manually, paste it from the email.

elements.name: Full Name field
elements.description: Enter your full name. This is how you will appear to other users in the system.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Set a password for your new account. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Create Account button
elements.description: Submits the invitation token, your name, and your password to create your account. If successful, you will be redirected to Login after a moment.

common_tasks.task: Accept an invitation and create your account
steps: Click the invitation link in the email you received.
Confirm the token field is filled (it should be automatic).
Enter your full name.
Set a password that meets the requirements.
Click Create Account.
You will be redirected to Login — sign in with the email the invitation was sent to and your new password.

warnings: Invitation tokens expire after a set period. If your token has expired, ask the business owner to send a new invitation.
The email you use to log in after this step is the email the invitation was sent to — not one you enter on this page.
You cannot use this page to join a business if you already have an account. Contact the business owner to add you from Settings.

related_pages.reason: After creating your account, log in here.

entries.title: Login
entries.overview: This is the sign-in page. Enter your email and password to access your business account. If your account is linked to more than one business, you will be asked to choose which business to open.

before_you_start.text: You need a registered account. If you don't have one, sign up first.
If you were invited by a business, use the Accept Invite page instead.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Enter the email address you registered with.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Enter your account password.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Forgot password link
elements.description: Takes you to the Password Reset page where you can request a reset link by email.

elements.name: Sign In button
elements.description: Submits your email and password. If correct, you are taken directly to the Dashboard.

elements.name: Business selector
elements.description: Appears only if your email is linked to more than one business. Choose which business you want to open.
elements.notes: The system remembers the last business you used and pre-selects it for you.

elements.name: Continue button
elements.description: Appears after the business selector. Confirms your business choice and completes the login.

elements.name: Create Business link
elements.description: Takes you to the Sign Up page if you want to register a new business account.

common_tasks.task: Log in to your account
steps: Enter your email address.
Enter your password.
Click Sign In.
If prompted, select your business and click Continue.

warnings: If you enter the wrong password too many times, your account may be temporarily locked.
If you belong to multiple businesses, you must select one to proceed — you cannot be logged into more than one business at the same time.

related_pages.reason: Email verification is required before you can log in for the first time.
First-time login redirects here to complete business setup.
You land here after a successful login.

entries.title: Notes
entries.overview: A structured note-taking tool for recording observations, decisions, and reminders that are linked directly to business records. Notes can be scoped to a branch or kept private, tagged for searchability, and linked to any entity in the system — from a product variant to a transfer order. Each note can have one or more timed reminders that fire via in-app notification, email, or WhatsApp. Use notes to leave context on supplier issues, flag a product for review, or record instructions for your team.

elements.name: Total notes KPI
elements.description: The total count of notes loaded under the current filters. Includes both active and archived notes if the archived filter is applied.

elements.name: Active KPI
elements.description: Count of notes that have not been archived. This is the working set of notes that appear in the default list view.

elements.name: With links KPI
elements.description: Count of notes that have at least one entity link attached. Linked notes are easier to surface when browsing the related record (e.g. a note linked to a supplier appears in that supplier's context).

elements.name: Branch scope KPI
elements.description: Shows how many notes in the current view are scoped to a specific branch (visibility = BRANCH). Use the Branch filter to narrow to a single branch's notes.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filters notes by keyword, searching the title and body text. Results update as you type. Combine with Tag or Visibility filters for precise searching.

elements.name: Tag filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to notes that carry a specific tag. Tags are free-text labels you assign when creating a note — consistent tagging (e.g. 'follow-up', 'urgent', 'seasonal') makes this filter powerful.

elements.name: Visibility filter
elements.description: Shows only notes with a selected visibility level: PRIVATE (yours alone), BRANCH (shared with a specific branch), or BUSINESS (visible to all users in the business). PRIVATE notes are hidden from everyone else including admins.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts the list to notes scoped to a specific branch. Only relevant when the Visibility filter is set to BRANCH or left unfiltered.

elements.name: Title input
elements.description: A short headline summarising the note. Appears prominently in the note card and in search results — make it descriptive enough to identify the note without opening it.

elements.name: Body textarea
elements.description: The full content of the note. Supports multi-line text. There is no hard length limit, but keep notes focused — long, unfocused notes are hard to scan.

elements.name: Visibility selector
elements.description: Controls who can read the note: PRIVATE — only you; BRANCH — all users with access to the selected branch; BUSINESS — all users in the business. PRIVATE notes are truly private — not even admins can read them.

elements.name: Branch selector (note form)
elements.description: Only active when Visibility is set to BRANCH. Determines which branch's users can see this note. If left blank when BRANCH is selected, the note will default to your current branch.

elements.name: Tags input
elements.description: Enter one or more tags as a comma-separated list (e.g. 'urgent, supplier-A, Q1'). Tags are free-form — there is no predefined list. The Tag filter on the notes list uses these exact values, so consistency matters.

elements.name: Link type selector
elements.description: Choose the type of entity to link this note to: Product, Variant, Branch, Supplier, Customer, PurchaseOrder, Purchase, or Transfer. Once a link is added, the note can surface contextually when viewing that record.

elements.name: TypeaheadInput (links)
elements.description: After selecting a link type, search for the specific record by name or ID. Results appear as you type — select one to stage it for linking.

elements.name: Add Link button
elements.description: Confirms the staged entity and adds it as a link chip on the note. A note can have multiple links — repeat the process to add more.

elements.name: Link chips
elements.description: Displays the entities currently linked to this note as removable chips. Each chip shows the entity type and name. Click the × on a chip to remove that link before saving.

elements.name: Save Note / Update Note button
elements.description: Creates the note (or saves changes to an existing one). After saving, the note appears in the list immediately and any linked entities will show this note in their context panels.

elements.name: View/Edit button (per note)
elements.description: Opens the note form pre-filled with the existing content, ready to edit. After making changes, click 'Update Note' to save. PRIVATE notes can only be edited by their creator.

elements.name: Archive button (per note)
elements.description: Moves the note to an archived state and removes it from the default list view. The note is not deleted — you can surface it again using the Visibility or Tag filters if you know it exists.

elements.name: Reminder panel
elements.description: Attach one or more timed reminders to the note. Pick a date and time, then select which channels should fire: IN_APP (notification inbox), EMAIL, or WHATSAPP. Click 'Add Reminder' to queue it. Reminders fire once at the scheduled time — they do not repeat.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (more detail per note) and table layout (more notes per screen).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered page navigation. The list loads 25 notes per page.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'notes.write' permission to create or edit notes.
To link a note to an entity (e.g. a supplier), that record must already exist in the system.
PRIVATE notes are only visible to you — not even admins can read them. Choose BUSINESS or BRANCH if you want to share.

common_tasks.task: Create a note linked to a supplier
steps: Enter a title and body describing the issue or observation.
Set visibility to BUSINESS or BRANCH as appropriate.
In the Links section, select 'Supplier' as the link type.
Type the supplier's name in the typeahead and select the result.
Click 'Add Link', then 'Save Note'.

common_tasks.task: Add a reminder to an existing note
steps: Find the note in the list and click 'View/Edit'.
Open the Reminder panel.
Pick a date and time using the DateTimePicker.
Select the desired notification channels (IN_APP, EMAIL, and/or WHATSAPP).
Click 'Add Reminder', then 'Update Note'.

common_tasks.task: Find all urgent notes for a specific branch
steps: Set the Tag filter to 'urgent' (or your team's equivalent tag).
Set the Branch filter to the branch in question.
Review results — use ViewToggle to see more notes per screen if needed.

warnings: PRIVATE notes are visible only to you — even business owners and admins cannot read them. Do not use PRIVATE for notes that your team needs to act on.
Archived notes disappear from the default list. They are not deleted, but there is no 'archived' view toggle — you would need to know the note exists to find it via search.
Reminders fire once at the scheduled time and do not repeat. If you need recurring reminders, create multiple reminder entries.

entries.title: Notifications
entries.overview: Your personal inbox for all system-generated notifications. Messages arrive in real time via a server-sent events stream — no manual refresh needed. Notifications are prioritised into four levels: ACTION_REQUIRED (something needs your response), WARNING (a threshold or rule was breached), INFO (routine updates), and SECURITY (login events, permission changes, or suspicious activity). Security alerts are surfaced in a separate red-bordered section at the top so they are never missed. Use bulk actions to keep the inbox clean.

elements.name: Inbox size KPI
elements.description: Shows how many notifications are currently in your inbox. This count includes read and unread items — use the Unread KPI to focus on what still needs attention.
