elements.name: Unread KPI
elements.description: Count of notifications you have not yet opened. When this is non-zero, the bell icon in the navigation bar also shows a badge. Reading or bulk-marking notifications as read decrements this counter.

elements.name: Action required KPI
elements.description: Count of notifications with priority ACTION_REQUIRED — these represent tasks that are blocked until you act, such as an approval waiting for your decision or a sync conflict that needs resolution. Aim to keep this at zero.

elements.name: Selected KPI
elements.description: Updates live as you check notification checkboxes, showing how many items are currently selected for a bulk action. Helpful for confirming your selection before clicking Mark Selected Read or Archive Selected.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filters your inbox by keyword, matching against the notification title and message body. Useful for finding a specific alert when you remember part of the wording but not the date.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Limits the list to UNREAD or READ notifications only. Use UNREAD to quickly focus on items you have not seen, or READ to review past messages.

elements.name: Priority filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to a single priority level: ACTION_REQUIRED, WARNING, INFO, or SECURITY. Combine with the Status filter to find, for example, all unread SECURITY notifications.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restricts results to notifications received within a specific window. Helpful when investigating an incident — filter to the date of the event to see exactly what was triggered.

elements.name: Select All checkbox
elements.description: Checks every notification currently visible in the list. If filters are active, only filtered items are selected — not your entire inbox. Use before Mark Selected Read or Archive Selected to act on a filtered set.

elements.name: Mark Selected Read button
elements.description: Marks all checked notifications as read in one action. The Unread KPI and navigation badge both update immediately. Does not archive — items remain in the inbox.

elements.name: Archive Selected button
elements.description: Removes all checked notifications from your inbox view. Archived notifications are no longer shown by default. Use this to declutter after you have dealt with a batch of alerts.

elements.name: Mark All Read button
elements.description: Marks every notification in your inbox as read — regardless of any active filters. This clears the Unread KPI and the navigation badge entirely. Use it after reviewing a busy inbox to start fresh.

elements.name: Security alerts section
elements.description: A visually distinct panel with a red border that shows only SECURITY priority notifications. It appears at the top of the page before the main notification list, ensuring security events are always visible even when the inbox is full. Security alerts include new login detections, permission changes, and failed authentication attempts.

elements.name: Notification item
elements.description: Each notification shows a priority badge (colour-coded), a title, the message body, and a relative timestamp (e.g. '2 hours ago'). Unread items are visually highlighted. Some notifications include a link directly to the relevant record (e.g. an approval request).

elements.name: Mark Read button (per item)
elements.description: Marks a single notification as read without affecting any other items. The item loses its unread highlight and the Unread KPI decrements by one.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered page navigation for large inboxes. The inbox loads 25 items per page by default.

before_you_start.text: No special permissions are required — every user has a personal notification inbox.
The real-time stream reconnects automatically if the connection drops. Notifications sent during a gap are delivered on reconnect.
Security alerts should be reviewed as soon as possible — they may indicate unauthorised access.

common_tasks.task: Clear all unread notifications
steps: Click 'Mark All Read' to clear the entire inbox at once.
Alternatively, filter by Status = UNREAD, select all with the Select All checkbox, then click 'Mark Selected Read'.

common_tasks.task: Find and respond to an action-required notification
steps: Use the Priority filter and select ACTION_REQUIRED.
Review each item — many include a direct link to the relevant page (e.g. an approval).
Click the link or navigate to the relevant module to complete the action.

common_tasks.task: Archive old notifications after a busy period
steps: Set a date range to isolate the period you want to clean up.
Click 'Select All' to check all visible items.
Click 'Archive Selected'.

warnings: Security alerts appear in a dedicated section — do not dismiss them without investigation, as they may indicate account compromise.
Archiving is not reversible from the UI — archived notifications are removed from the default inbox view.
Mark All Read affects all notifications, not just the currently filtered view — use carefully if you want to keep some items unread.

entries.title: Offline Mode
entries.overview: Register this device for offline operation so it can process sales, issue receipts, and record transfers even when there is no internet connection. Offline actions are stored in a local queue on the device and automatically synced back to the server when connectivity is restored. A PIN can be configured to allow secure local login when the server is unreachable. Subscription limits apply: your plan defines the maximum duration you can operate offline, the maximum number of sales, and the maximum total transaction value — check Offline limits info to see what applies to your account.

elements.name: Device name input
elements.description: A human-readable label for this specific device (e.g. 'Main Counter Tablet', 'Warehouse POS'). This name appears in the server's device registry and in audit logs, making it easy to trace which device performed an offline action.

elements.name: Register Device button
elements.description: Registers this device with the server for offline use. After registration, the device receives an offline token that allows local authentication and operation without an active internet connection. Requires the device to be online at the time of registration.

elements.name: Revoke Device button
elements.description: Revokes this device's offline registration and permanently clears all locally stored offline data, including the queue of unsynced actions. Only use this after syncing — any pending actions in the queue will be lost when the device is revoked.

elements.name: PIN input
elements.description: Enter a 4–8 digit numeric PIN. This PIN is stored securely on the device and used to authenticate offline login when the server cannot be reached. Choose a PIN that is not used for any other account or service.

elements.name: Set PIN button
elements.description: Saves the entered PIN to the device's local secure storage. From this point on, users can log in offline using this PIN instead of their normal credentials. Changing the PIN replaces the previous one immediately.

elements.name: Clear PIN button
elements.description: Removes the stored offline PIN from this device. After clearing, offline login will not be possible until a new PIN is set. Use this if a staff member who knew the PIN has left or if you suspect the PIN was compromised.

elements.name: Sync Now button
elements.description: Manually triggers a sync of the offline queue to the server. Normally sync happens automatically when the device reconnects, but you can force it here to confirm the queue is clear before going offline again. The pending actions count should drop to zero after a successful sync.

elements.name: Pending actions count
elements.description: The number of offline actions (sales, transfers, etc.) queued locally and not yet sent to the server. This should reach zero after every sync. A persistently non-zero count indicates sync is blocked — check the Sync blocked warning.

elements.name: Queue stats panel
elements.description: Displays current queue usage: number of items and total payload size in bytes, compared against the maximum values allowed by your subscription. If you approach the limit, sync before continuing to operate offline.

elements.name: Sync blocked warning
elements.description: Appears when automatic sync has been paused — typically because a previous sync attempt produced unresolvable conflicts. While sync is blocked, new offline actions still queue but do not sync. Go to the Offline Conflicts page to resolve the blocking conflicts.

elements.name: Last sync timestamp
elements.description: Shows the date and time of the most recent successful sync. If this timestamp is very old and the pending count is non-zero, the device may have been offline for an extended period — ensure sync completes before approaching your subscription's offline duration limit.

elements.name: Receipt history table
elements.description: Lists receipts that were issued offline. Each row shows the local receipt number assigned at the time of the sale and the official server-assigned receipt number once the action was synced. Use this to look up a receipt issued during an outage.

elements.name: Device status
elements.description: Shows the server-side status of this device's registration: ACTIVE (registered and permitted to operate offline), REVOKED (registration was manually revoked), or EXPIRED (the offline token exceeded the subscription's maximum offline duration).

elements.name: Offline limits info
elements.description: Displays the offline constraints defined by your subscription plan: maximum continuous offline duration (hours), maximum number of sales allowed while offline, and maximum total transaction value allowed while offline. Exceeding these limits will cause the offline token to expire.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'offline.write' permission to register or configure offline mode.
Your subscription must include offline access — if the Register button is disabled, check Business Settings → Subscription.
The device must be online at least once to register and to sync the queue. Offline mode only works after initial registration.

common_tasks.task: Register this device for offline use
steps: Make sure you are connected to the internet.
Enter a descriptive name for this device in the Device name input.
Click 'Register Device'.
Device status will change to ACTIVE — the device can now operate offline.

common_tasks.task: Set an offline PIN
steps: Enter a 4–8 digit PIN in the PIN input.
Click 'Set PIN'.
Test the PIN by logging out and attempting an offline login.

common_tasks.task: Force-sync the offline queue
steps: Ensure the device is online.
Click 'Sync Now'.
Wait for the sync to complete — the Pending actions count should reach zero.
If the count does not reach zero, check the Sync blocked warning and resolve conflicts.

warnings: Revoking a device clears all locally stored data and the entire unsynced queue — ensure the queue is empty (Pending = 0) before revoking.
If sync is blocked, new actions continue to queue but nothing will be sent to the server until conflicts are resolved on the Offline Conflicts page.
Do not use the same PIN as any bank card, email, or other account — the PIN is stored locally and protects access to business data.

entries.title: Offline Conflicts
entries.overview: When the offline queue is synced back to the server, each action is re-validated against current business rules. If a rule is violated — for example, a sale was made at a price outside the allowed variance, or an action required manager approval — it is rejected and appears here as a conflict. Each conflict must be explicitly resolved: retried, overridden, sent for approval, or dismissed. Unresolved conflicts block automatic sync for new actions, so clear this page promptly after going back online.

elements.name: Total conflicts KPI
elements.description: The number of offline actions that failed server-side validation and are waiting for resolution. This number must reach zero before automatic sync can resume normally.

elements.name: Approval Required KPI
elements.description: Count of conflicts where the action needed manager approval at the time it was processed. These cannot be auto-applied — a manager must approve them via the Approvals module.

elements.name: Price Variance KPI
elements.description: Count of conflicts where a sale was processed offline at a price that falls outside the configured acceptable variance from the product's current price. These can be overridden (force through the offline price) or dismissed.

elements.name: Conflict card
elements.description: Summarises a single failed action: the action type (e.g. SALE, TRANSFER), the conflict reason (PRICE_VARIANCE, APPROVAL_REQUIRED, or other), the server's error message explaining why it was rejected, and the full original payload so you can review exactly what was attempted.

elements.name: Retry button
elements.description: Re-submits the action to the server with no modifications. This succeeds only if the underlying rule violation has since been resolved (e.g. the price range was widened by a manager). If the same rule still applies, the retry will fail again.

elements.name: Override Price button
elements.description: Only available for PRICE_VARIANCE conflicts. Forces the action through using the offline price, bypassing the price variance rule. This is recorded in the audit log. Use only when you are certain the offline price was correct and authorised.

elements.name: Sync Approval button
elements.description: Only available for APPROVAL_REQUIRED conflicts. Moves the action into the Approvals queue so a manager can review and approve it. The action will be applied once approved, or discarded if rejected.

elements.name: Dismiss button
elements.description: Permanently discards this conflict and the underlying action. The sale, transfer, or other record it represents will not be created. This cannot be undone — only dismiss if you are certain the action should be abandoned.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next batch of conflict records to the list. Appears when there are more conflicts than the initial page load.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'offline.write' permission to view and resolve conflicts.
The device must be online — this page cannot be used while offline.
Conflicts appear only after a sync attempt fails. If there are no conflicts shown, your offline queue synced cleanly.

common_tasks.task: Resolve a price variance conflict
steps: Open the conflict card and review the payload — check what item was sold and at what price.
If the offline price was authorised, click 'Override Price' to force it through.
If the sale should not be recorded, click 'Dismiss' to abandon the action.

common_tasks.task: Send an offline sale for manager approval
steps: Find the conflict with reason APPROVAL_REQUIRED.
Click 'Sync Approval' to move it to the Approvals queue.
Notify the relevant manager to review the approval in the Approvals module.

warnings: Dismissed conflicts are permanently lost — the action will not be recorded anywhere in the system. Do not dismiss without careful review.
Override Price bypasses pricing rules and is logged in the audit trail — only use it when explicitly authorised.
Resolving all conflicts is required to unblock automatic sync. Until this page is clear, new offline actions queue but do not sync.

entries.title: Business Setup
entries.overview: This guided setup runs once the first time you log in after registering. It walks you through 3 steps: setting up your business profile, configuring your first branch, and optionally inviting your team. You cannot skip to the main system until both the business profile and branch setup are marked complete.

before_you_start.text: You must have verified your email address before reaching this page.
You need the business.update and settings.write permissions to save changes. As the business owner, you have these by default.

elements.name: Step indicator pills
elements.description: Three pills at the top show your progress: Business Profile → Branch Setup → Team. The currently active step is highlighted.

elements.name: Business Name field (Step 1)
elements.description: Your business name. Pre-filled from registration — update it here if you want to change it.

elements.name: Currency field (Step 1)
elements.description: The currency your business operates in. Default is TZS. Enter the 3-letter currency code (e.g. TZS, USD).

elements.name: Timezone field (Step 1)
elements.description: Your business timezone. This is read-only and set to Africa/Dar_es_Salaam. Contact support if you need to change it.

elements.name: Date Format field (Step 1)
elements.description: Controls how dates are displayed throughout the system on receipts, reports, and all date fields. Set this to your country's standard (e.g. DD/MM/YYYY for Tanzania) before inviting staff — it affects how all dates are read and entered across the platform.

elements.name: Save & Continue button (Step 1)
elements.description: Saves your business name, contact details, currency, and date format, then advances to Step 2 (Branch Setup). You can return to these settings any time via Business Settings → Profile.

elements.name: Branch list (Step 2)
elements.description: Shows all branches currently created for your business. A badge marks the default branch.

elements.name: Branch selector (Step 2)
elements.description: Select one of the pre-created branches to review or update its details. Your first branch was created automatically when you signed up — select it here to set its display name, address, and contact phone.

elements.name: Branch Name / Address / Phone fields (Step 2)
elements.description: The branch's display name appears in reports, receipts, and navigation menus. Address and phone are optional but print on receipts if provided. Set these accurately now — staff and customers will see this information on every receipt.

elements.name: Save Branch Details button (Step 2)
elements.description: Saves the name, address, and phone for the selected branch and marks it as configured. You can add more branches later in Settings → Branches.

elements.name: Add Another Branch section (Step 2)
elements.description: A separate form to create a new branch. Fill in Name (required), Address, and Phone, then click Add Branch.

elements.name: Next button (Step 2)
elements.description: Marks branch setup as complete and moves you to Step 3. You must have at least one branch before this button works.

elements.name: Open Users button (Step 3)
elements.description: Navigates to Settings → Team Members where you can invite staff by email and assign roles. Complete roles setup first so each person can be assigned the correct access level from day one.

elements.name: Open Roles button (Step 3)
elements.description: Navigates to Settings → Roles where you can create custom roles and configure permission sets. Set up your roles before inviting users — without roles configured, invited staff will have no permissions.

elements.name: Finish Later button (Step 3)
elements.description: Skips the team setup step and takes you directly to the Dashboard. You can invite team members any time from Settings.

common_tasks.task: Complete business setup
steps: Step 1: Check and update your Business Name, Currency, and Date Format, then click Save & Continue.
Step 2: Review or edit your default branch details. Add more branches if needed, then click Next.
Step 3: Click Open Users to invite staff, or click Finish Later to go to the Dashboard.

warnings: You cannot proceed to the system until Step 1 (Business Profile) and Step 2 (Branch Setup) are both marked complete.
At least one branch is required before you can complete Step 2.
The Timezone field is read-only. If your timezone is wrong, contact support.
If the system shows a Read Only banner, your account is temporarily restricted. Contact support.

workflow.step: Step 3: Open Users/Roles or finish later, then route back to app dashboard.
workflow.expected_result: Onboarding exits cleanly and app access continues with setup flags persisted.

common_errors.likely_cause: Branch setup completion was submitted with zero active/non-archived branches.
fix_steps: Create at least one branch before pressing next on branch setup.
Reload branch list and verify the branch is not archived.
Retry onboarding completion after branch exists.

common_errors.error_symptom: Onboarding save actions fail with PERMISSION_IS_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: User role lacks required permissions for business/settings update actions.
fix_steps: Ask admin to grant business.update and settings.write permissions.
Retry onboarding action after permission update takes effect.
If role is branch-scoped, verify scope allows current setup actions.

common_errors.likely_cause: Session lost business context or token/session state is stale.
fix_steps: Re-login to refresh session and business context.
Confirm active business is selected before returning to onboarding.
Retry onboarding save operations after session refresh.

related_pages.reason: Onboarding starts after successful Login when setup is incomplete.
Business settings is a direct follow-up for deeper policy/profile adjustments.
Branch settings is the operational follow-up for managing branch records.
Users setup is the next step for team configuration after onboarding core setup.

entries.title: Reset Password
entries.overview: Use this page if you have forgotten your password. Enter your email address and the system will send you a password reset link. If your email is linked to more than one business, you will need to choose which business to reset the password for.

before_you_start.text: You need access to the email address you registered with.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Enter the email address your account is registered under.

elements.name: Business selector
elements.description: Appears only if your email is linked to more than one business. Choose which business you want to reset the password for.
elements.notes: This appears after you submit your email the first time.

elements.name: Send Reset Link button
elements.description: Submits your email and sends a password reset link to your inbox. Check your email (and spam folder) after clicking.

common_tasks.task: Request a password reset
steps: Enter your email address.
Click Send Reset Link.
If prompted, choose your business from the dropdown and click Send Reset Link again.
Check your inbox for the reset email and click the link inside it.

warnings: The reset link expires after a short time. Use it promptly after it arrives in your email.
If you do not receive the email within a few minutes, check your spam or junk folder.
This page only sends the reset link — to actually set your new password, follow the link in the email.

related_pages.reason: You arrive here from Login when you forget your password.
After clicking the email link, you land here to set your new password.

entries.title: Set New Password
entries.overview: This page is where you create your new password after clicking the reset link in your email. The reset token is filled in automatically from the link. Enter your new password, then submit.

before_you_start.text: You must have received and clicked the password reset link sent to your email.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The reset token from your email. Usually filled in automatically when you arrive via the email link. If it is blank, paste the token from the email manually.

elements.name: New Password field
elements.description: Your new password. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below the field.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your new password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Set Password button
elements.description: Submits the token and new password. If successful, you will be redirected to the Login page after 1.5 seconds.

common_tasks.task: Set your new password
steps: Confirm the token field is filled (it should be automatic).
Type your new password.
Click Set Password.
You will be redirected to Login — sign in with your new password.

warnings: Reset tokens expire after a short time. If you see a token expired error, go back to the Password Reset page and request a new link.
Your new password must meet the minimum requirements. A password hint is shown below the field.
After setting your new password, you will be redirected to Login automatically.

related_pages.reason: You must request a reset link here before you can use this page.
After setting your new password, sign in here.

entries.title: Point of Sale (POS)
entries.overview: This is the live checkout screen. You scan or search for products, build a cart, apply discounts, and collect payment. When you complete a sale the system deducts stock, issues a receipt, and records the transaction. Works both online and offline — if you lose connection, sales are queued and sync automatically when you reconnect.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Scan Panel)
elements.description: Choose which branch you are selling from. This determines which stock is deducted. Locked once items are in the cart.
elements.notes: You cannot change branch mid-sale. Empty the cart first if you need to switch.

elements.name: Customer Selector (optional)
elements.description: Attach a customer to this sale. Optional — leave blank for walk-in customers. Required if you want to track credit balances per customer.

elements.name: Product Search / Barcode Input
elements.description: Type a product name, SKU, or barcode and press Enter or click Add. The system will find the matching variant and add it to the cart. Supports typeahead suggestions as you type.

elements.name: Add Button
elements.description: Adds the searched or scanned product to the cart. Same as pressing Enter in the search field.

elements.name: Start / Stop Scan Button
elements.description: Activates the device camera to scan barcodes. Click again to stop. Automatically picks the rear camera if available.
elements.notes: Browser must be granted camera permission. If denied, you'll see a message — use text search instead.

elements.name: Product Panel
elements.description: Shows all available products in a grid. Click any card to instantly add it to the cart. Filter using the search box at the top of the panel.

elements.name: Cart Items
elements.description: Each item in the cart shows the product name, quantity input, unit selector, unit price input, line discount input, and a Remove button. Edit any field directly.
elements.notes: If a price list is active, unit price is pre-filled. Min price warnings show in red if you go below the limit.

elements.name: Cart Discount
elements.description: Enter a flat discount amount to apply across the entire cart. This is separate from per-item line discounts.

elements.name: Totals Panel
elements.description: Shows Subtotal, Discounts (line + cart), VAT, and Total. Updates live as you edit the cart.

elements.name: Payment Method Selector
elements.description: Choose how the customer is paying: Cash, Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer, or Other. You can add multiple payment rows for split payments.

elements.name: Payment Amount Input
elements.description: Enter the amount for each payment method. For cash, you can enter more than the total — the system calculates change.

elements.name: Add Payment Button
elements.description: Adds another payment row to the payment section, enabling split payments across multiple methods (e.g. part cash, part mobile money). Each row has its own method selector and amount field — total must equal the sale amount to complete.

elements.name: Credit Sale Checkbox
elements.description: When checked, allows completing the sale with partial or no payment — the balance is recorded as a customer credit debt. Only visible if credit sales are enabled in Business Settings.
elements.notes: Requires the customer to be selected and a credit due date to be set.

elements.name: Credit Due Date
elements.description: Appears when Credit Sale is checked. Set the date by which the customer should settle the balance.

elements.name: Complete Sale Button
elements.description: Finalises the sale. Deducts stock, issues a receipt, and records all payments. If offline, the sale is queued for later sync.

elements.name: Connect Printer Button
elements.description: Connects to a USB/Bluetooth ESC/POS receipt printer. Only needed if you use a hardware printer.

elements.name: Hardware Print Toggle
elements.description: When enabled, receipts print automatically to the connected hardware printer after each completed sale.

elements.name: Print Last Receipt Button
elements.description: Reprints the receipt from the most recently completed sale to the connected hardware printer.

elements.name: Preview & Print Button
elements.description: Opens a receipt preview modal with Compact and Detailed view options. From the preview you can print using the browser print dialog.

before_you_start.text: You need at least one active product variant with a selling price in the catalog.
Your role needs the sales.write permission. Without it, the Complete Sale button is disabled.
If shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, you must open a shift for your branch before you can complete sales.

common_tasks.task: Process a standard sale
steps: Select your branch from the Branch Selector.
Optionally select a customer.
Search for products or scan barcodes to build the cart.
Adjust quantities, unit, unit price, or line discounts as needed.
In the Totals panel, enter payment amount and method.
Click Complete Sale — the receipt is issued automatically.

common_tasks.task: Process a sale offline
steps: The POS detects no internet connection automatically.
If an offline PIN is required, enter it in the PIN field that appears.
Build and complete the sale as normal.
The sale is queued with a local receipt number — it syncs when you go back online.

warnings: You cannot change the branch once items are in the cart. Empty the cart first if you need to switch branches.
For non-cash payments (Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer), the payment total must exactly match the sale total — there's no change calculation for non-cash. Use Cash for overpayment.
If shift tracking is turned on in Business Settings and no open shift exists for your branch, the Complete Sale button will be blocked.
Large discounts may trigger an approval workflow — the sale will not complete immediately if approval is required.
Offline sales are queued locally. Do not close the browser or clear site data before syncing, or queued sales may be lost.

related_pages.reason: Open a shift here before starting POS if shift tracking is enabled.
Check available stock before a sale to avoid stock-out failures at checkout.
View, reprint, and process refunds on completed sales receipts.
Controls credit enablement, shift tracking, VAT rate, and receipt template used by POS.

entries.title: Price Lists
entries.overview: Create and manage named price lists that override your default product pricing. Each list can contain custom prices for individual product variants. You can assign a price list to a branch (so all sales at that branch use the custom prices) or to a customer (so that customer always gets their negotiated prices, regardless of branch). Use the Price Lists Wizard for bulk percentage or flat-amount adjustments across an entire list.

elements.name: Name input (create form)
elements.description: Enter a unique name for the new price list — for example 'Wholesale' or 'VIP Customers'. The name must be unique within your business and cannot be blank.

elements.name: Create button
elements.description: Creates the price list with ACTIVE status and adds it to the list immediately. You can start adding variant price overrides to it right away using the Add Item form below.

elements.name: Price list card
elements.description: Shows the list name, its current status (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED), and how many variant price overrides it contains. An ACTIVE list affects pricing wherever it is assigned. Click Edit to change the name or status.

elements.name: Edit button (per list)
elements.description: Puts the list card into edit mode, enabling the name and status fields. Other users can still read the list while you are editing.

elements.name: Status selector (edit mode)
elements.description: ACTIVE — the list is applied to any branch or customer it is assigned to. INACTIVE — the list exists but pricing is not applied even where assigned, useful for pausing a list temporarily. ARCHIVED — hides the list from selection menus and stops all pricing application.

elements.name: Save / Cancel (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits your name or status changes immediately. Cancel reverts all fields to the last saved state without making any changes.

elements.name: Add Item form
elements.description: Use this to add a price override for a specific variant on a specific list. Select the target list, choose the variant, and enter the custom price, then click Add. The override is saved and takes effect immediately wherever the list is assigned.

elements.name: Price list selector (item form)
elements.description: Choose which price list this variant price override belongs to. All your existing price lists are shown here.

elements.name: Variant selector (item form)
elements.description: SmartSelect showing all product variants in your catalogue. Search by product name, variant name, or SKU to find the right one.

elements.name: Price input (item form)
elements.description: The custom price to charge for this variant when the list is active. Enter the amount in your business currency. This replaces the variant's default price wherever this list is applied — it does not affect the default price.

elements.name: Remove item button
elements.description: Permanently removes the variant's price override from the list. The variant immediately reverts to its default price at any branch or customer using this list.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of price list cards when you have more lists than the initial display.

before_you_start.text: You need the price-lists.manage permission to create, edit, or delete price lists and their items.
Products and variants must already exist in the catalogue before you can add items to a price list. Create products first from the Catalog page.
To link a price list to a branch or customer, do that from the Branches or Customers page — it cannot be done from this page.

common_tasks.task: Create a new price list
steps: Enter a name in the Name input at the top of the page.
Click Create. The list appears in the list below with ACTIVE status.
Use the Add Item form to begin adding variant price overrides to the new list.

common_tasks.task: Add a variant price override to a list
steps: In the Add Item form, select the target price list from the Price list selector.
Search for and select the variant using the Variant selector.
Enter the custom price in the Price input.
Click Add. The override is saved and applied immediately wherever the list is assigned.

common_tasks.task: Deactivate a price list temporarily
steps: Find the list card and click Edit.
Change Status to INACTIVE.
Click Save. The list is preserved with all its overrides but stops affecting pricing at branches or for customers.

warnings: Setting a price list to ARCHIVED hides it from all selection menus. Any branch or customer already assigned to an archived list stops receiving custom pricing and falls back to default prices immediately.
Creating a price list does not automatically apply it anywhere. You must go to the Branches or Customers page to assign the list — otherwise it has no effect on sales.
For bulk adjustments (e.g. increase all prices by 10%), use the Price Lists Wizard instead of editing overrides one by one.

entries.title: Price Lists Wizard
entries.overview: Bulk-update prices across an entire price list in four guided steps: choose scope, define the adjustment type and value, preview every change, then apply. Supports three modes — percentage change, flat amount add/subtract, or fixed price override — and can target either all product variants or only those already on the list. Use this when you need to update many prices at once, such as applying an annual price increase or adjusting for a new cost.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your current position across the four wizard steps: Scope → Adjust → Preview → Apply. Completed steps show a checkmark. You can click Back at any step to change earlier selections without losing later ones.

elements.name: Price list selector (Scope step)
elements.description: Choose which price list to update. Only ACTIVE or INACTIVE lists appear — ARCHIVED lists cannot be updated through the wizard.

elements.name: Scope selector
elements.description: ALL adds or updates every variant in your catalogue on this list, including variants not yet on it. EXISTING updates only variants that already have a price override on the chosen list. Use EXISTING if you do not want to add new variants to the list.

elements.name: Mode selector (Adjust step)
elements.description: PERCENT — multiply each current price by a percentage change (e.g. +10 for a 10% increase, -5 for a 5% reduction). ADD — add a flat amount to each price (use a negative number to reduce). SET — replace all affected prices with exactly the value you enter, regardless of the current price.

elements.name: Value input
elements.description: The numeric value for the adjustment. For PERCENT, enter the percentage (e.g. 10 means +10%). For ADD, enter the flat amount in your business currency (negative values reduce prices). For SET, enter the exact price all affected variants will be set to.

elements.name: Preview table
elements.description: Shows every affected variant with its current price and the calculated new price after the adjustment. Review this carefully before applying — once you click Apply, there is no undo. If any prices look wrong, click Back to adjust your settings.

elements.name: Show More button (Preview)
elements.description: Loads additional rows in the preview table when there are more variants than the initial display. Scroll through the full list before applying.

elements.name: Apply button
elements.description: Permanently commits all price changes to the selected list. Cannot be undone. Only click after reviewing the full preview table and confirming every price looks correct.

elements.name: Back / Next buttons
elements.description: Move between wizard steps. Back returns you to the previous step without losing your current step's settings. Next validates the current step before proceeding.

before_you_start.text: You need the price-lists.manage permission.
A price list must already exist. Create one from the Price Lists page first.
If using EXISTING scope, the list must already have at least one variant price override on it — otherwise nothing is updated.

common_tasks.task: Increase all prices on a list by 10%
steps: Select the price list on the Scope step.
Choose ALL scope to include every variant in your catalogue.
Click Next. On the Adjust step, select PERCENT mode and enter 10.
Click Next. Review the Preview table row by row to confirm the new prices are correct.
Click Apply to commit the changes permanently.

common_tasks.task: Reduce existing list items by a flat amount
steps: Select the list and choose EXISTING scope, then click Next.
Select ADD mode and enter the amount as a negative number (e.g. -500 to reduce each price by 500).
Click Next, review the Preview, then click Apply.

warnings: Applying the wizard is permanent and cannot be undone. Take note of current prices or export them before running the wizard if you need a backup.
ALL scope with SET mode will set every variant in your catalogue to the same fixed price — this is almost never what you want. Use EXISTING scope or PERCENT/ADD mode to avoid unintended overwrites.
Percentage adjustments on very small prices may produce unexpected results due to rounding. Always review the full Preview table before clicking Apply.

entries.title: Purchase Orders
entries.overview: This page lets you create formal orders to request stock from suppliers. Each order lists what you're ordering, quantities, costs, and expected delivery date. Orders can go through an approval flow before they are sent for receiving. The system can also suggest order lines automatically based on low-stock reorder points.

elements.name: Open Orders KPI
elements.description: Shows the total number of purchase orders currently loaded (based on active filters).

elements.name: Pending Approval KPI
elements.description: Count of purchase orders waiting for a manager to approve before they can proceed to receiving. Orders in this state cannot be sent to the supplier or actioned by receiving staff. If this number is persistently high, there may be an approval bottleneck — check who has approval authority.

elements.name: Approved / Active KPI
elements.description: Count of orders that are approved, partially received, or fully received — orders actively in progress or recently completed. Use this to understand how many open orders your team is currently working through.

elements.name: Due in 7 Days KPI
elements.description: Count of orders with an expected delivery date within the next 7 days. Use this as a daily prompt to follow up with suppliers or prepare your receiving team for incoming goods. Click through to filter the list to these orders.

elements.name: Open Wizard Link
elements.description: Opens the step-by-step Purchase Order Wizard — a guided alternative to the inline create form.

elements.name: Search / Filters Bar
elements.description: Filter orders by keyword, status, branch, supplier, or date range. Click Show Advanced for date filters.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Select which branch this order is for. Stock received against this order will go to this branch.

elements.name: Supplier Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Select the supplier you're ordering from. Only active suppliers appear. If a supplier has a lead time set, the system shows an estimated delivery date.

elements.name: Expected Date (Create form)
elements.description: Optional. Set the date you expect delivery. If the selected supplier has a lead time configured, the system suggests a date automatically.

elements.name: Use Reorder Suggestions Button
elements.description: Auto-fills the order lines with products that have fallen below their reorder point for the selected branch. Only active if the branch is selected.
elements.notes: This replaces the current draft lines. Review and adjust quantities before submitting.

elements.name: Order Lines (Create form)
elements.description: Each line row has: Product variant selector, Quantity input, Unit selector, Unit Cost input, and a Remove button. Add as many lines as needed.

elements.name: Add Line Button (Create form)
elements.description: Adds a new empty product line row to the order. Each row represents one variant being ordered — enter the variant, quantity, unit price, and expected unit for each line before saving.

elements.name: Create Button
elements.description: Submits the new purchase order. If your business requires approval, the order is set to Pending Approval status.

elements.name: Order Selector (Update form)
elements.description: Select an existing purchase order to edit. Only DRAFT orders can be freely edited — editing an approved order may trigger a new approval request.

elements.name: Update Button
elements.description: Saves edits to the selected purchase order. Only available for orders in DRAFT or APPROVED status — orders that have begun receiving cannot have line items changed.

elements.name: Approve Button (per order card)
elements.description: Approves a purchase order that is in Pending Approval status. If the approval workflow requires a manager sign-off, a request is sent instead.
elements.notes: Requires purchases.write permission. Disabled if you lack the permission.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card view and table view for the orders list.

before_you_start.text: You need at least one active supplier set up before creating purchase orders.
Your role needs the purchases.write permission to create, update, or approve orders.
To use reorder suggestions, products in your catalog must have reorder points configured.

common_tasks.task: Create a purchase order
steps: Select a branch and supplier in the Create Order form.
Optionally set an expected delivery date.
Add line items — choose variant, enter quantity and unit cost.
Or click Use Reorder Suggestions to auto-fill low-stock items.
Click Create. If approval is required, the order goes to Pending Approval.

common_tasks.task: Approve a purchase order
steps: Find the order in the list — it will show a Pending Approval badge.
Click the Approve button on the order card.
If your role can approve directly, status changes to Approved. Otherwise an approval request is submitted.

warnings: You cannot remove lines from a purchase order once it has been saved — you can only reduce the quantity. Plan your lines carefully before creating.
Each variant can only appear once per order. Duplicate variants in the same order will cause the update to fail.
Editing an approved order may trigger the approval workflow again and put it back into Pending Approval status.
The supplier must be active (not archived) to create or update an order against them.

related_pages.reason: Set up and activate suppliers here before creating purchase orders.
After an order is approved, go here to record the stock you actually received.

entries.title: Purchase Order Wizard
entries.overview: A 4-step guided form to create a purchase order and optionally receive the goods in the same session. The wizard covers: selecting a supplier and branch, adding order lines (product, quantity, unit cost), reviewing totals, then recording received quantities with optional batch codes and expiry dates. If the order total triggers an approval threshold, the PO is created but stock is held until an approver acts.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your current position across the four steps: Details → Lines → Review → Receive. Completed steps show a checkmark. You can click Back to return to a previous step before the PO is created — once you click Create PO on the Review step, earlier steps are locked.

elements.name: Branch selector (Details step)
elements.description: The branch where the ordered goods will be received. Stock is added to this branch when the order is received. You cannot change the branch after the PO is created — choose carefully.

elements.name: Supplier selector
elements.description: The supplier this order is being placed with. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear. If the supplier has a lead time configured, the Expected date field will show a suggested ETA automatically.

elements.name: Expected date
elements.description: The date you expect goods to arrive. If the selected supplier has a lead time set, a suggested ETA (today + lead time days) appears automatically — adjust it if your agreed delivery date is different. This date is informational and does not trigger automatic actions.

elements.name: Line row (Lines step)
elements.description: One line per product being ordered. For each line, choose the variant (what you are ordering), enter the quantity, the unit cost (the price your supplier charges per unit), and the unit of measure. Add as many lines as needed for a single supplier order.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds a new blank line to the order. Use this when ordering multiple products from the same supplier in a single purchase order — one line per variant.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that line from the order before it is created. Once the PO is created on the Review step, lines cannot be removed.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Displays a full summary of all order lines with quantities, unit costs, line totals, and the overall order total. Review every line carefully — clicking Create PO on this step creates the purchase order permanently and locks all lines.

elements.name: Create PO button
elements.description: Creates the purchase order in the system. After clicking, the PO is locked — quantities, costs, and lines cannot be changed. The wizard advances automatically to the Receive step.

elements.name: Receive step
elements.description: After the PO is created, record the actual quantities delivered. The ordered quantities are pre-filled — adjust each to match what actually arrived. If goods are partially delivered, enter only the received amounts; the remaining balance stays open on the PO for a future receiving session.

elements.name: Batch code input (per line)
elements.description: Record the batch or lot number from the supplier's packaging. Required if batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Batch codes let you trace exactly which batch of stock was sold or returned later — critical for perishable goods and regulated items.

elements.name: Expiry date input (per line)
elements.description: Record the expiry date for perishable or regulated items (food, medicines, chemicals). Required if the variant has expiry tracking enabled. Items with different expiry dates from the same delivery should be split into separate lines.

elements.name: Receive button
elements.description: Records all entered quantities as received and updates stock levels at the destination branch immediately. If the PO total triggers an approval threshold, the approval banner appears and stock is held until an approver acts — clicking Receive still submits the request.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the PO total exceeds the approval threshold configured in Business Settings. The PO is created successfully, but stock will not be updated until a user with the approvals.write permission approves it on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Back / Next buttons
elements.description: Navigate between wizard steps. Back returns to the previous step without losing your current step's data. Note: once you click Create PO on the Review step, you cannot go back to change order lines.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
The supplier and destination branch must already exist — create them first if needed.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings if you need to record batch codes and expiry dates on received goods.

common_tasks.task: Create a purchase order
steps: On the Details step, select the branch where goods will be received, choose the supplier, and set the expected delivery date. Click Next.
On the Lines step, add one line per product: select the variant, enter quantity, unit cost, and unit of measure. Click Next.
On the Review step, check all quantities, costs, and the total. Click Create PO to lock the order.
The wizard advances to the Receive step — receive immediately or skip to receive later from the Receiving page.

common_tasks.task: Receive goods immediately after creating the PO
steps: After creating the PO, you are on the Receive step automatically.
Adjust each quantity to match what actually arrived (the ordered quantity is pre-filled).
If batch tracking is enabled, enter the batch code and expiry date for each line.
Click Receive. Stock levels at the branch update immediately.

common_tasks.task: Handle a partial delivery
steps: On the Receive step, enter only the quantities that actually arrived for each line — leave lines at 0 if nothing arrived for that item.
Click Receive to update stock for the delivered portion.
Return to the Receiving page later to record the remaining goods when they arrive — the PO stays open.

warnings: If an approval is triggered, stock will not be added to the branch until the approval is granted on the Approvals page. The PO shows as PENDING in the meantime.
Unit cost entered here forms the cost basis for stock valuation and profit calculations in reports. Enter the correct supplier price — incorrect values affect margin reporting.
The PO cannot be edited after creation. If you made an error (wrong supplier, wrong branch, wrong cost), contact your supervisor — the PO may need to be voided and a new one raised.

entries.title: Purchases
entries.overview: This page is where you record what you bought from suppliers — the invoice, what products were included, how much you paid, and how you paid. You can also record partial payments and come back later to settle the balance. Works offline: purchases recorded without internet sync automatically when you reconnect.

elements.name: Total Purchases KPI
elements.description: Count of purchase records in the current filtered view. Apply date filters to understand purchasing volume over a period — for example, compare this month against last month to track buying activity trends.

elements.name: Draft Lines KPI
elements.description: Shows how many product lines are in the current new purchase form. Resets to 1 after each successful create.

elements.name: Payment Target KPI
elements.description: Shows whether a purchase is currently selected in the Record Payment form. 'None' means no purchase is targeted yet.

elements.name: Search / Filters Bar
elements.description: Filter purchases by keyword, status (DRAFT, APPROVED, RECEIVED, etc.), branch, supplier, and date range.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Select the branch this purchase was made for. Stock movements are tied to this branch.

elements.name: Supplier Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Select the supplier you bought from. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear in this dropdown. If your supplier is missing, create or reactivate them on the Suppliers page before returning here.

elements.name: Purchase Lines (Create form)
elements.description: Each row has: Product variant, Quantity, Unit, Unit Cost, and a Remove button. Add as many rows as there are products in the invoice.

elements.name: Add Line Button
elements.description: Adds a new product line row to the purchase. Each row represents one variant with its quantity and unit cost. A purchase must have at least one line before it can be saved.

elements.name: Create Purchase Button
elements.description: Saves the purchase record and immediately creates PURCHASE_IN stock movements for each line item, increasing stock on hand at the selected branch. If offline, the record is queued locally and stock updates when synced.

elements.name: Purchase Selector (Record Payment form)
elements.description: Choose which existing purchase you want to record a payment against. Shows supplier name and status.

elements.name: Payment Method Selector
elements.description: Choose how the payment was made: Cash, Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer, or Other.

elements.name: Amount Input
elements.description: Enter the payment amount. Can be partial — you can record multiple payments against the same purchase over time.

elements.name: Reference Input (optional)
elements.description: Enter a transaction reference number. Required for Bank Transfer and Mobile Money payments.

elements.name: Method Label Input (optional)
elements.description: For the 'Other' payment method, enter a label describing the payment type (e.g., 'Cheque').

elements.name: Record Payment Button
elements.description: Saves a payment against this purchase and reduces the outstanding balance. If the payment equals the total cost, the purchase is marked PAID. Partial payments are recorded as instalments — remaining balance is visible on the purchase card.

elements.name: Purchases List
elements.description: Shows all purchases in card or table view. Each card shows supplier, status, date, total, and any recorded payments.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card and table layout for the purchases list.

before_you_start.text: You need at least one active supplier. Set up suppliers before recording purchases.
Your role needs the purchases.write permission to create purchases or record payments.

common_tasks.task: Record a supplier invoice
steps: Select a branch and supplier in the Create Purchase form.
Add a line for each product: select the variant, enter quantity and unit cost.
Click Create Purchase. The purchase is saved with a DRAFT status.

common_tasks.task: Record a payment against a purchase
steps: In the Record Payment form, select the purchase from the Purchase Selector.
Choose the payment method and enter the amount.
For Bank Transfer or Mobile Money, enter the reference number.
Click Record Payment.

warnings: Bank Transfer and Mobile Money payments require a reference number — the form will warn you if it's missing.
The supplier must be active to create a purchase against them. Archived suppliers won't appear in the selector.
Purchases created offline are queued and synced when you reconnect — do not clear browser storage before syncing.

related_pages.reason: Set up your suppliers here before recording purchases.
Stock levels update when purchases are received — check here after posting.

entries.title: Receipts
entries.overview: View the full history of sales receipts across all branches. From this page you can find a specific receipt, preview and reprint it, process a refund for a completed sale, settle a customer's outstanding credit balance, or handle a return when the original receipt cannot be found. Each action is permission-controlled — viewing is open to all, but refunds, credit settlement, and no-receipt returns each require specific permissions.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Find receipts by receipt number, customer name, or product name. Use this when a customer brings in a receipt and you need to pull up the original sale quickly.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restrict results to receipts from a specific branch. Use this when you are a branch manager reviewing your branch's sales, or when a customer made their purchase at a different branch and you need to filter to that location.

elements.name: Customer filter
elements.description: Show all receipts for a specific customer. Useful when reviewing a customer's full purchase history, looking for a sale to refund, or finding all sales to credit against a customer's account.

elements.name: Variant filter
elements.description: Show only receipts that included a specific product variant. Useful for tracing how many units of a particular item were sold, or for returns investigations on a specific product.

elements.name: User filter
elements.description: Show receipts processed by a specific staff member. Use this for end-of-shift reconciliation or reviewing a cashier's session totals.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restrict results to a specific time window. Combine with Branch or User filter for shift-end or daily reconciliation.

elements.name: Receipt card / row
elements.description: Shows the receipt number, date and time of the sale, total amount, customer name (if a customer was selected at POS), branch, and current status (COMPLETED, REFUNDED, PARTIAL_REFUND, etc.). Click a receipt to open its full detail and available actions.

elements.name: Receipt preview
elements.description: Opens a formatted view of the receipt showing all line items, quantities, prices, discounts applied, payment method and amount, and totals. This is the same layout used when printing — use it to verify the receipt details before reprinting or refunding.

elements.name: Reprint button
elements.description: Sends the receipt to the connected thermal printer (ESC/POS protocol). If no printer is connected or configured, the receipt preview still opens and can be printed through the browser's print function as a fallback.

elements.name: Refund button
elements.description: Initiates a refund for the selected sale. You are prompted to choose which line items to refund (full or partial) and the refund payment method. On confirmation, the payment is reversed and the returned items are added back to stock at the branch where the sale occurred. Requires the sales.write permission.

elements.name: Settle Credit button
elements.description: Records a cash payment received from a customer against their outstanding credit balance. Use this when a customer who previously bought on credit comes in to pay off what they owe. Requires the sales.credit.settle permission.

elements.name: Return Without Receipt button
elements.description: Processes a product return when the original receipt genuinely cannot be found. You manually select the items being returned and enter their details. Use this as a last resort only — it bypasses normal receipt validation, which can create stock discrepancies if misused. Requires the sales.return.without-receipt permission.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (more detail per receipt) and table layout (better for scanning large volumes of receipts at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of receipts. Apply filters first to narrow the result set — searching an unfiltered full receipt history can return thousands of records.

before_you_start.text: No special permission is required to view receipts.
The sales.write permission is required to process refunds.
The sales.credit.settle permission is required to settle a customer's credit balance.
The sales.return.without-receipt permission is required to process returns without the original receipt.

common_tasks.task: Reprint a receipt
steps: Search for the receipt by number or customer name.
Click the receipt to open its detail, then click Reprint.
The receipt is sent to the connected printer. If no printer is connected, use the browser's print function from the preview.

common_tasks.task: Process a refund
steps: Find and open the original receipt using the search or filters.
Click Refund. Select which line items to refund and choose the refund payment method.
Confirm the refund. Payment is reversed and returned items are added back to stock at the original branch.
A refund receipt is generated automatically and can be printed.

common_tasks.task: Settle a customer's outstanding credit
steps: Search for a receipt for the customer, or use the Customer filter to find their records.
Click Settle Credit on any of their receipts.
Enter the amount being paid and confirm. The customer's outstanding credit balance reduces immediately.

warnings: Refunds are irreversible — once confirmed, the payment is reversed and stock is returned. Verify you have the correct receipt and the correct lines selected before confirming.
Settling credit updates the customer's balance immediately and cannot be undone. Double-check the payment amount before confirming.
Return Without Receipt bypasses normal receipt validation. Only use it when the receipt genuinely cannot be found — misuse can create stock discrepancies that are difficult to trace and correct.

entries.title: Receiving
entries.overview: Record the arrival of goods against an existing purchase order or purchase. Use this page when goods arrive after you have already created the PO — as opposed to receiving goods immediately inside the Purchase Order Wizard. Supports partial deliveries (receive what arrived, leave the rest open), batch code tracking, and expiry date recording for perishable or regulated items.

elements.name: Link type selector
elements.description: Choose whether to receive goods against a Purchase Order or a Purchase. This controls which documents appear in the Document selector below. Most receiving is done against a Purchase Order.

elements.name: Document selector
elements.description: Select the specific purchase order or purchase you are receiving goods against. Only documents in a receivable state appear (e.g. approved POs with outstanding quantities). The document's supplier, branch, and order lines load automatically once you select it.

elements.name: Receiving line row
elements.description: One row per item being received. Shows the variant being received, a quantity field (pre-filled with the outstanding ordered quantity), the unit cost, and the unit of measure. Optionally shows batch code and expiry date fields if batch tracking is enabled. Adjust the quantity to match what actually arrived.

elements.name: Quantity received (per line)
elements.description: Enter the actual quantity delivered for this line. You can enter less than the outstanding quantity to record a partial delivery — the remaining balance stays open on the PO for a future receiving session. Enter 0 if nothing arrived for that item.

elements.name: Batch code input (per line)
elements.description: Enter the batch or lot number from the supplier's packaging label. Required if batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Batch codes let you trace which specific batch of stock was sold, transferred, or returned — critical for perishable goods and regulated items.

elements.name: Expiry date input (per line)
elements.description: Enter the expiry date for perishable or regulated items. Required if the variant has expiry tracking enabled. Stock with different expiry dates from the same delivery should be split into separate receiving lines so each batch has its own expiry recorded.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds a new receiving line for an additional item. Use this if you need to receive a variant that was not on the original purchase order — for example, a bonus item or a substitution sent by the supplier.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that line from the receiving session before you submit. Use this to correct a mistake — lines cannot be removed after submission.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Records all receiving lines and updates stock levels at the branch immediately. This action cannot be undone — verify all quantities, batch codes, and expiry dates before clicking Submit.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the receiving total triggers an approval threshold configured in Business Settings. The receiving record is submitted, but stock levels are not updated until a user with the approvals.write permission approves it on the Approvals page.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
A purchase order or purchase must already exist and have outstanding quantities — you cannot receive against a PO that is already fully received or cancelled.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings before receiving if you need to record batch codes and expiry dates.

common_tasks.task: Receive a full delivery against a purchase order
steps: Set Link type to Purchase Order and select the PO from the Document selector.
The lines from the PO are pre-filled with the outstanding quantities. Verify each quantity matches what arrived.
If batch tracking is enabled, enter batch codes and expiry dates for each line.
Click Submit. Stock levels at the branch update immediately.

common_tasks.task: Record a partial delivery
steps: Select the PO from the Document selector.
For lines that arrived, enter the actual quantity received. For lines that did not arrive, enter 0 or leave them.
Click Submit. The received quantities are added to stock. The PO stays open with the remaining balance for a future receiving session.

common_tasks.task: Receive an unlisted or bonus item
steps: Select the related PO or Purchase from the Document selector.
Click Add Line and select the variant that arrived.
Enter the quantity, unit cost, and any batch details.
Click Submit to record the receiving and update stock.

warnings: Submitted receiving records update stock immediately and cannot be edited or deleted. Verify all quantities, batch codes, and expiry dates before clicking Submit.
Partial receiving leaves the PO open — come back to this page and select the same PO when the remaining goods arrive to complete the receiving.
Batch codes must be unique within a branch and variant combination. If you enter a duplicate batch code, the system will warn you before allowing submission.

entries.title: Reports
entries.overview: This route redirects automatically to the Reports Overview section — the entry point for the full analytics dashboard. The dashboard has five sections accessible via tabs: Overview (business summary KPIs and totals), Sales & Profit (revenue trends, gross margin, and top products), Customers (spending patterns, credit, and new vs returning), Inventory (stock values, movements, and low-stock alerts), and Operations (shift reconciliation, transfers, and approvals). Use the date range and branch filters on each section to focus the data.

elements.name: Automatic redirect
elements.description: Navigates you directly to the Reports Overview section on page load. No interaction is needed — the browser redirects automatically. If you want a specific section, use the section tabs once you land on the Reports dashboard.

before_you_start.text: You need the reports.read permission to access the Reports dashboard.

common_tasks.task: Go directly to a specific report section
steps: Navigate to Reports — the page redirects to the Overview section automatically.
Use the section tabs at the top of the dashboard to switch to Sales & Profit, Customers, Inventory, or Operations.

entries.title: Reports Dashboard
entries.overview: Business analytics dashboard with five reporting sections. Each section shows KPI cards and charts relevant to that area of the business. All data can be filtered by date range and branch. Use this page for performance reviews, end-of-period analysis, and operational monitoring.

elements.name: Section tabs
elements.description: Five tabs switch between reporting areas. Overview — business-wide summary of revenue, profit, transactions, and active customers for the period. Sales & Profit — revenue trends over time, gross margin analysis, top-selling products, and payment method breakdown. Customers — new vs returning customers, top customers by spend, outstanding credit balances, and customer activity trends. Inventory — current stock value by branch, stock movement summary (sales out, stock received, adjustments, transfers), and low-stock alerts. Operations — shift variance summary, transfer activity and status, approval queue totals, and expense breakdown by category.

elements.name: Date range filter
elements.description: Sets the reporting period for all KPI cards and charts on the current section. Changing the date range reloads all data. Common presets include Today, This Week, This Month, and Last Month — or set a custom From/To range for any period.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts all report data to a specific branch. If no branch is selected, data from all branches is aggregated into a business-wide view. Use this to compare branches or review a specific location's performance.

elements.name: KPI cards (Overview section)
elements.description: Summary metrics for the selected period: total revenue, gross profit, net transactions, average sale value, and number of active customers. Use these as the headline figures for any performance review.

elements.name: KPI cards (Sales & Profit section)
elements.description: Revenue and margin metrics: total sales by period, gross margin percentage, cost of goods sold, and revenue from top product categories.

elements.name: KPI cards (Customers section)
elements.description: Customer activity metrics: total customers, new customers in the period, returning customer rate, and total outstanding credit balance across all customers.

elements.name: KPI cards (Inventory section)
elements.description: Stock health metrics: total stock value across all branches, number of variants with low or zero stock, total stock received in the period, and total stock adjustments.

elements.name: KPI cards (Operations section)
elements.description: Operational metrics: total shift variance (cash discrepancy across all closed shifts), number of pending approvals, total transfers in transit, and total expenses for the period.

elements.name: Charts (per section)
elements.description: Visual breakdowns that complement the KPI cards. Examples: revenue over time (line chart), top products by units sold (bar chart), stock movement categories (pie chart), customer spending tiers (bar chart). Charts update automatically when date range or branch filters change.

elements.name: Export report button
elements.description: Downloads the current section's data as a CSV file, reflecting the date range and branch filter currently applied. Available on most sections. Use this for sharing data with external tools or for deeper analysis in a spreadsheet.

before_you_start.text: You need the reports.read permission to access the Reports dashboard.
Your subscription tier must include access to reports — lower tiers may have restricted access to some sections.
Data reflects completed transactions only — pending, draft, or approval-held records are not included in report totals.

common_tasks.task: View sales summary for the current month
steps: Go to the Sales & Profit section tab.
Set the date range to This Month.
Review the KPI cards for total revenue and gross margin, and the charts for revenue trends and top products.

common_tasks.task: Check stock value by branch
steps: Go to the Inventory section tab.
Set Branch filter to the branch you want to review (or leave blank for all branches).
The Stock Value KPI card shows the current total inventory value for the selected branch.

common_tasks.task: Export a report section to CSV
steps: Navigate to the section you want to export.
Set the date range and branch filter as needed.
Click Export report. A CSV file downloads to your device with the section's data.

warnings: Reports show data from completed transactions only — real-time figures at the POS may differ slightly from what appears here until transactions are fully processed.
Changing the date range or branch filter reloads all data automatically — give the page a moment to update before reading figures.

entries.title: Search
entries.overview: Global search that queries products, variants, receipts, customers, and stock transfers simultaneously from a single input. Type at least two characters to see live auto-suggest results. Submit the search to see full results grouped by entity type. Each result links directly to the relevant record — clicking it navigates you to that page.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Type your search query here. After two characters, live suggestions appear showing matching variant names, SKUs, customer names, and receipt numbers. Press Enter or click Search to run a full search across all entity types.

elements.name: Search button
elements.description: Runs a full search for the current query across all entity types. Use this when you want complete results beyond the auto-suggest preview, or after refining your query.

elements.name: Total matches count
elements.description: Total number of records matched across all entity types combined. If this is very high, refine your query to be more specific — for example, include a SKU or receipt number.

elements.name: Products section
elements.description: Lists products whose name matches the query, showing each product's name and its variants. Click a product to navigate to its catalogue detail page where you can view pricing, stock levels, and settings.

elements.name: Variants section
elements.description: Lists product variants matching the query by SKU or variant name. Click a variant to open its detail page. Use this when you have a SKU from a physical label or a supplier document and need to find the exact variant in the system.

elements.name: Receipts section
elements.description: Lists receipts whose receipt number matches the query. Click a receipt to open its full detail page, where you can reprint it or initiate a refund.

elements.name: Customers section
elements.description: Lists customers whose name matches the query. Click a customer to open their profile, which includes their credit balance, purchase history, and contact details.

elements.name: Transfers section
elements.description: Lists stock transfer records matching the query, showing the source and destination branch names. Click a transfer to view its full detail and current status.

before_you_start.text: No special permissions are required to use Global Search — all users can access it.
Type at least 2 characters to activate the auto-suggest. Single-character queries return no results.

common_tasks.task: Find a product by name or SKU
steps: Type the product name or SKU into the search input.
Results appear in the Products section (by name) or Variants section (by SKU).
Click the result to navigate directly to the product or variant detail page.

common_tasks.task: Find and reprint a receipt by number
steps: Type the receipt number (or part of it) into the search input.
Click the receipt in the Receipts section.
The receipt detail page opens — use the reprint or refund options there.

warnings: Results are limited to your current business only — you cannot search across multiple businesses.

If your role restricts access to a module (e.g. you cannot access Customers), results from that module may still appear in search — but clicking them will show a permission error rather than the record.

entries.title: Branch Settings
entries.overview: Create and manage the physical locations (branches) of your business. Each branch has its own name, address, phone number, and optionally a price list that overrides your default product pricing for sales made at that location. Branches are fundamental to the system — stock levels, sales, transfers, shifts, and reports are all tracked per branch.

elements.name: Total branches KPI
elements.description: Total number of branches currently loaded. Your subscription plan sets a maximum branch limit — if you are near the limit, a usage warning appears in the Subscription panel on the Business Settings page.

elements.name: With price list KPI
elements.description: How many loaded branches have a price list assigned. Branches without a price list use your default product prices at POS. If this is lower than expected, check which branches still need a list assigned.

elements.name: More pages KPI
elements.description: Indicates whether additional branch records exist beyond the current page. Click Load More at the bottom to fetch the next batch.

elements.name: Can write KPI
elements.description: Confirms whether your current role has permission to create or edit branches. If this shows No, the Create Branch button and Edit controls are visible but disabled.

elements.name: Name input (create form)
elements.description: Required. The display name for the new branch — shown in branch selectors throughout the system including POS, stock movements, transfers, and reports. Must be unique within your business.

elements.name: Address input (create form)
