elements.description: Optional. The physical street address of the branch. Shown on printed receipts and in some reports.

elements.name: Phone input (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The branch's contact phone number. If entered, it prints on receipts issued at this branch and appears in the branch directory. Can be added or changed later by editing the branch record.

elements.name: Price List selector (create form)
elements.description: Optionally link an existing price list to this branch. When assigned, all sales made at this branch will use the custom prices from that list instead of default product prices. Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page.

elements.name: Create Branch button
elements.description: Saves the new branch immediately. The branch becomes available across the system — in POS branch selectors, stock management, transfer forms, and shift tracking — as soon as it is created.

elements.name: Branch card
elements.description: Displays the branch name, address, phone, and linked price list. One card per branch. If a field is blank, it was left optional during creation.

elements.name: Edit button (per branch)
elements.description: Enters edit mode for that branch card, enabling the name, address, phone, and price list fields for editing.

elements.name: Save / Cancel (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits your edits to the branch immediately. Cancel reverts all fields to the last saved state without making any changes.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of branch records when you have more branches than the initial display.

before_you_start.text: You need the business.write permission to create or edit branches.
Price lists must be created on the Price Lists page before they can be assigned to a branch here.

common_tasks.task: Create a new branch
steps: Enter the branch name in the Name input (required). Optionally add an address and phone.
Select a price list from the Price List selector if this branch uses custom pricing.
Click Create Branch. The branch is immediately available across the system.

common_tasks.task: Assign a price list to an existing branch
steps: Find the branch card and click Edit.
Select the price list from the Price List selector.
Click Save. New sales at this branch will now use the custom prices from the assigned list.

warnings: Branch names must be unique within your business. Duplicate names are rejected when you try to save.
You cannot delete a branch from this page. Branches with any transaction history cannot be deleted — contact support if a branch needs to be permanently removed or archived.
Changing a branch's price list takes effect on the next new sale, not retroactively. Past receipts at that branch are not affected.

entries.title: Business Settings
entries.overview: The central control panel for your business account. Configure approval workflows, stock policies, POS behaviour, locale settings, notification routing, and subscription management. Also provides tools to manage measurement units, review support access requests, and — if needed — permanently delete the business account.

elements.name: KPI — Business
elements.description: Displays your registered business name as stored in the system. This is the name printed on receipts, exports, and customer-facing documents. Update it in the Business Profile section below if it is incorrect.

elements.name: KPI — Subscription
elements.description: Your current subscription status: ACTIVE (fully operational), TRIAL (limited free access period), GRACE (payment overdue but still accessible for a grace window), or EXPIRED (access restricted). If the status is GRACE or EXPIRED, go to Subscription Settings to renew.

elements.name: KPI — Roles
elements.description: Total number of roles defined in this business. A quick reference when planning user access — if you need a new role type, create it in Settings → Roles before inviting staff.

elements.name: KPI — Users
elements.description: Total number of user accounts in this business across all statuses (active, pending, deactivated). Useful for checking against your subscription's user limit if one applies.

elements.name: Business profile card
elements.description: Shows your business name and the internal business ID used in API calls and support requests. The ID is read-only and cannot be changed — share it with support when reporting an issue.

elements.name: Subscription panel
elements.description: Shows the current subscription tier, status, expiry/trial/grace dates, subscription warnings, and usage bars for users, branches, products, and offline devices.

elements.name: Subscription request form
elements.description: Lets you request a plan change (Upgrade, Downgrade, or Cancel) by selecting a type, target tier, and optional reason. Requires 'subscription.request' permission.

elements.name: Subscription request history
elements.description: Lists previous subscription change requests with their status and any admin response notes.

elements.name: Units management — Label / Code / Type fields
elements.description: Create custom measurement units (e.g. 'Kilogram / KG / WEIGHT') used across inventory and sales. Requires 'settings.write' permission.

elements.name: Create Unit button
elements.description: Saves the new unit to the business unit catalogue, making it available in the Base Unit and Sell Unit selectors on the Variants page. Duplicate unit names are rejected — search for the unit before creating to avoid doubles.

elements.name: Edit button (settings)
elements.description: Switches all settings sections on this page to edit mode, unlocking all input fields. No changes are saved until you click Save. You can cancel at any point to discard all unsaved changes and revert to the last saved state.

elements.name: Save Settings button
elements.description: Saves all draft settings changes to the server. Requires 'settings.write' permission. Browser warns if you try to navigate away with unsaved changes.

elements.name: Cancel button (settings)
elements.description: Closes edit mode and discards all unsaved changes to business settings, reverting every field to its last saved value. Use this if you started editing but want to back out without saving anything.

elements.name: Approval defaults section
elements.description: Toggle approval requirements for stock adjustments, refunds, purchases, transfers, and expenses. Set monetary thresholds — operations below the threshold proceed without approval.

elements.name: Discount threshold settings
elements.description: Set the maximum discount percentage or amount a cashier can apply without an approval.

elements.name: Stock policies section
elements.description: Configure whether negative stock is allowed, the batch dispatch order (FIFO/FEFO), inventory valuation method (FIFO/LIFO/AVERAGE), expiry handling policy (ALLOW/WARN/BLOCK), expiry alert days, batch tracking toggle, transfer batch policy, and low stock threshold.

elements.name: POS policies section
elements.description: Configure receipt template (Thermal/A4), receipt header and footer text, whether branch contact is shown on receipts, credit sales toggle, shift tracking toggle, shift variance threshold, discount limits, refund stock return default, offline price variance tolerance, and offline session limits (max hours, max sales, max value).

elements.name: Locale settings section
elements.description: Set the business currency code, timezone, and date format used throughout the application.

elements.name: Notification defaults section
elements.description: Configure default notification recipients (global, email, WhatsApp) and which notification events are enabled by default across the business.

elements.name: Support access requests panel
elements.description: Lists pending and resolved requests from the platform support team to access your account. You can approve or reject each request.

elements.name: Delete Business panel
elements.description: Permanently deletes the entire business account and all its data. Requires 'business.delete' permission and a triple confirmation (business ID, account password, typing 'DELETE').

before_you_start.text: Requires 'settings.write' permission to edit or save any settings.
Requires 'subscription.request' permission to submit subscription change requests.
Requires 'business.delete' permission to access the delete business panel.
The browser will warn you if you navigate away with unsaved settings changes.
Setting up approval thresholds requires understanding your operational workflows — ask your accountant or operations manager before enabling approvals.
Stock valuation method changes affect how the system calculates cost of goods — consult your accountant before changing this.

common_tasks.task: Enable approval for large purchases
steps: Click 'Edit' to enter settings edit mode.
In Approval Defaults, toggle 'Purchase approval' on.
Set the threshold amount — purchases above this value will require approval.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Change the receipt footer message
steps: Click 'Edit' to enter settings edit mode.
In POS Policies, update the 'Receipt footer' field.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Add a custom unit of measure
steps: In the Units section, enter the label (e.g. 'Kilogram'), code (e.g. 'KG'), and select the unit type.
Click 'Create Unit'.

common_tasks.task: Request a subscription upgrade
steps: In the Subscription Requests section, select 'Upgrade' as the type.
Select the target tier.
Optionally enter a reason.
Click 'Send Request'. The platform team will review and respond.

common_tasks.task: Approve a support access request
steps: Scroll to the Support Access Requests panel.
Review the request details (reason, scope, duration).
Click 'Approve' or 'Reject'.

warnings: Deleting the business is irreversible — all data including sales history, stock records, and user accounts will be permanently erased.
Changing the stock valuation method (FIFO/LIFO/AVERAGE) affects cost calculations for all future stock movements — this should only be changed in consultation with your accountant.
Enabling BLOCK expiry policy will prevent selling expired batches — ensure your team is aware before activating this.
Unsaved settings changes will be lost if you navigate away without clicking 'Save Settings' (the browser will warn you first).
Offline limits (max hours, max sales, max value) cap what POS devices can do while disconnected — set them too low and cashiers may be blocked during a connectivity outage.

entries.title: My Profile
entries.overview: View your personal account details, the roles assigned to you, and every permission you currently hold within the business. If a feature is blocked for you, check the Permissions panel here to see if the required permission is missing — then use the Request Access form to ask an administrator for it.

elements.name: Roles KPI
elements.description: The number of roles currently assigned to you. Each role grants a bundle of permissions. If this shows 0, you likely have very limited access to the system — contact your administrator.

elements.name: Permissions KPI
elements.description: Total number of individual permissions you hold across all your roles. Use this as a quick reference — if a feature is blocked and this count seems low, you may be missing a required permission.

elements.name: Status KPI
elements.description: Your account status: ACTIVE means you have normal access. SUSPENDED means your account has been temporarily disabled by an administrator — contact them to resolve it.

elements.name: Membership KPI
elements.description: Your membership status within this business. ACTIVE means you are a current team member. PENDING means your invitation has not yet been fully accepted or confirmed.

elements.name: Profile card
elements.description: Read-only display of your full name, email address, phone number, account status, last login timestamp, and account creation date. To change any of these details, contact your administrator — they cannot be edited from this page.

elements.name: Roles panel
elements.description: Lists each role assigned to you with its name and branch scope (if applicable). A branch-scoped role means your access is restricted to data from that specific branch only — you cannot see other branches' sales, stock, or reports.

elements.name: Permissions panel
elements.description: All permissions you hold displayed as labelled chips, grouped by module. If a button or page is blocked for you, check here first — the permission you need should be visible if you have it, or absent if you do not.

elements.name: Permission selector (Request Access form)
elements.description: Lists only the permissions you do not currently hold. Select the one you need. If the permission you are looking for is not in the list, you may already have it — check the Permissions panel above.

elements.name: Reason input (Request Access form)
elements.description: Explain why you need this permission. A specific, clear reason helps the administrator approve your request faster. Example: 'I need to process refunds for customers who contact our support line.'

elements.name: Submit button (Request Access form)
elements.description: Sends your access request to an administrator with the approvals.write permission. You will receive a notification when the request is approved or rejected.

before_you_start.text: All users can view their own profile — no special permission is required.
Access requests require approval from a user with the approvals.write permission. If no one in your business has this permission, requests cannot be processed — contact support.

common_tasks.task: Check which permissions you have
steps: Open the Permissions panel on this page.
Browse the chips grouped by module. If a specific permission is missing, it will not appear here — use the Request Access form below.

common_tasks.task: Request a new permission
steps: Select the permission you need from the Permission selector in the Request Access form.
Enter a clear, specific reason in the Reason input explaining your use case.
Click Submit. An administrator will be notified and will approve or reject your request.

warnings: You cannot edit your own profile details (name, email, phone) from this page. Contact your administrator to update your account information.
Access requests go to administrators with the approvals.write permission. If your request sits unanswered, check with your administrator or contact support if no one has that permission.

entries.title: Roles
entries.overview: Defines the permission sets that control what each team member can do across the system. The business ships with system roles (read-only presets like Cashier, Manager, Viewer) and you can create unlimited custom roles for your specific workflows. Each role is a collection of individual permissions — for example, 'catalog.create' or 'stock.adjust'. The permission editor groups permissions by module and provides Read / Standard / Full presets so you can configure a role quickly without selecting every checkbox individually. Permission changes take effect immediately on the next request made by any user holding that role.

elements.name: KPI — Visible Roles
elements.description: Count of roles shown in the current filtered view. Without any filters applied, this is the total number of roles in the business.

elements.name: KPI — Permissions
elements.description: Total number of individual permission codes available in the system. This is the full catalogue from which any role's permission set is drawn.

elements.name: KPI — Selected Role
elements.description: Displays the name of the role currently open in the permission editor. Shows '—' when no role is being edited. Keeps you oriented when the editor is open alongside a long roles list.

elements.name: KPI — Assigned Permissions
elements.description: Count of permissions currently checked for the selected role. Updates live as you toggle checkboxes in the editor — before saving you can see exactly how many permissions the role will have.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filters the role list by name as you type (350 ms debounce). Useful in businesses with many custom roles.

elements.name: Scope filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to system roles (built-in, cannot be deleted) or custom roles (created by your business). System roles can have their permissions edited but cannot be renamed or removed.

elements.name: Permission count filter
elements.description: Filter to roles that have at least one permission assigned, or to roles with no permissions at all. Roles with no permissions assigned are effectively inactive — users holding them can log in but cannot do anything.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all search and filter inputs and reloads the full role list.

elements.name: Role name field (create form)
elements.description: Enter a descriptive name for the new custom role (e.g. 'Branch Cashier', 'Stock Auditor'). The name appears in the user management page and in audit logs.

elements.name: Create button
elements.description: Creates the new role with the provided name and zero permissions. After creation, click 'Edit Permissions' on the new role to configure its permission set. Requires 'roles.create' permission.

elements.name: Edit Permissions button (per role)
elements.description: Opens the permission editor panel for this role. The System Owner role is locked and cannot be edited. Requires 'roles.update' permission.

elements.name: Permission search (editor)
elements.description: Filters the permission list by permission code, title, or description. Use this to find a specific permission when you know its name but not which module it belongs to.

elements.name: Module filter (editor)
elements.description: Narrows the permission editor to show only permissions from one module (e.g. catalog, stock, sales, users). Combine with the module presets to configure one module at a time.

elements.name: Read preset button (per module)
elements.description: Checks only the view/list/read permissions for the selected module, clearing any write or delete permissions that were previously checked. Use this for read-only access to a specific area.

elements.name: Standard preset button (per module)
elements.description: Checks the read permissions plus standard write permissions for the module, excluding high-risk operations like delete, anonymise, or force-close. This is the recommended starting point for most operational roles.

elements.name: Full preset button (per module)
elements.description: Checks every permission for the module, including high-risk operations. Review the risk level badges before applying — Full preset is appropriate only for senior staff who need complete control over that area.

elements.name: Permission checkboxes (editor)
elements.description: Individual toggles for each permission. Each checkbox shows the permission title, a risk level badge (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH), a description of what it allows, and the raw permission code. Read descriptions carefully before checking high-risk permissions.

elements.name: Permission guide panel
elements.description: A read-only reference panel explaining what the Read, Standard, and Full preset levels mean and what risk levels are associated with each. Useful when onboarding team members who are new to managing roles.

elements.name: Save Permissions button
elements.description: Saves the current checkbox state as the complete permission set for this role. This replaces the role's entire permission set — any permissions not checked will be removed. Changes take effect immediately: users holding this role gain or lose access on their next request. Requires 'roles.update' permission.

elements.name: Close button (editor)
elements.description: Closes the permission editor without saving. Any unsaved checkbox changes are discarded.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Fetches the next page of 50 roles and appends them to the list.

before_you_start.text: Requires 'roles.create' permission to create new roles.
Requires 'roles.update' to edit a role's permissions.
The System Owner role is permanently locked — it cannot be edited or deleted.
Permission changes take effect immediately — existing sessions for users with this role will reflect changes on their next server request.

common_tasks.task: Create a new role
steps: Enter a descriptive role name in the create form.
Click 'Create'. The role is created with no permissions.
Click 'Edit Permissions' on the new role to configure its access.

common_tasks.task: Configure a cashier role with POS access and read-only catalog
steps: Create a role named 'Cashier' (or open an existing one).
Click 'Edit Permissions'.
Use the Module filter to select 'catalog', then click the 'Read' preset.
Switch the Module filter to 'sales', then click the 'Standard' preset.
Review the checked permissions, then click 'Save Permissions'.

common_tasks.task: Audit which permissions a role has
steps: Click 'Edit Permissions' on the role.
Scroll through the list or use the Permission search to inspect individual permissions.
The KPI — Assigned Permissions shows the total count.
Close the editor without saving if you made no intentional changes.

warnings: Saving permissions replaces the role's entire permission set — permissions that are unchecked will be removed immediately.
The Full preset includes high-risk permissions (delete, anonymise, force-close shifts) — review risk badges before applying.
The System Owner role cannot be modified. Changes to all other roles, including system presets, take effect immediately.

entries.title: Team Members
entries.overview: Manage everyone who has access to your business account. Invite new staff by email, configure their notification preferences (including which WhatsApp language they receive messages in), and control their access by assigning roles. Roles can be scoped to a specific branch — useful for cashiers or branch managers who should only see their own branch's data. Deactivating a user blocks all login immediately without deleting their history.

elements.name: KPI — Visible Users
elements.description: Count of users shown in the current list, respecting any active filters. The full user count (no filters) tells you the total team size.

elements.name: KPI — Active
elements.description: Count of users with ACTIVE status — those who can currently log in. Comparing this to Visible Users reveals how many accounts are suspended, pending, or deactivated.

elements.name: KPI — Roles
elements.description: Total number of roles defined in the business. A quick reference to know how many roles are available before inviting a new user.

elements.name: KPI — Branches
elements.description: Total number of branches in the business. Relevant when assigning branch-scoped roles — reminds you how many branches exist so you can set up access correctly.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filters the user list by name or email address as you type (350 ms debounce). Useful for finding a specific person quickly in a large team.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to users with a specific account status: ACTIVE (can log in), SUSPENDED (temporarily blocked), DEACTIVATED (permanently blocked), or PENDING (invited but has not yet completed sign-up).

elements.name: Role filter
elements.description: Shows only users who have a specific role assigned. Useful for auditing who holds a high-privilege role, or for finding all cashiers in the system.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active filters and reloads the full user list.

elements.name: Email field (invite form)
elements.description: The email address of the person you want to invite. They will receive an invitation email with a secure sign-up link. The email must not already be registered in the system.

elements.name: Role selector (invite form)
elements.description: Assigns an initial role to the invited user. The System Owner role is not available here — it can only be transferred via account settings. You can adjust the role after the user accepts the invitation.

elements.name: Send Invite button
elements.description: Sends the invitation email and creates a PENDING user record. The invitation expires after 48 hours — if the person does not accept in time, you will need to send a new invite. Requires 'users.create' permission.

elements.name: Edit button (per user)
elements.description: Opens an inline edit form for the user's profile. You can change their display name, email address, phone number, account status, WhatsApp notification language, and which notification event types they receive. Requires 'users.update' permission.

elements.name: Deactivate button (per user)
elements.description: Blocks the user from logging in immediately. Their historical data (sales, transfers, audit logs) is preserved. The user can be reactivated later by editing their status back to ACTIVE. Requires 'users.deactivate' permission.

elements.name: Roles button (per user)
elements.description: Opens the Role Assignments panel for this user, showing all roles currently assigned and whether each is global or branch-scoped. From here you can add new role assignments or remove existing ones.

elements.name: Name / Email / Phone / Status fields (edit mode)
elements.description: Editable profile fields. Status can be toggled between ACTIVE and DEACTIVATED here as an alternative to the dedicated Deactivate button.

elements.name: WhatsApp locale selector (edit mode)
elements.description: Sets the language for WhatsApp notification messages sent to this user: English or Swahili. This affects only WhatsApp messages — in-app and email notifications follow the system locale.

elements.name: Notification event checkboxes (edit mode)
elements.description: Individual toggles for each notification event type (e.g. sale approved, low stock alert, shift started). Unchecking an event type means this user will not receive notifications for that event, regardless of channel.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits all profile changes and closes the edit form. Cancel discards changes and returns to the list view without saving anything.

elements.name: Role Assignments panel — Role selector
elements.description: Selects the role to assign to this user from the business's role catalogue. After choosing a role, optionally set a branch scope before clicking Assign Role.

elements.name: Role Assignments panel — Branch selector
elements.description: Optionally scopes the role assignment to a specific branch. If a branch is selected, the user's permissions from this role only apply to that branch's data. Leave blank to assign the role globally across all branches.

elements.name: Assign Role button
elements.description: Adds the selected role (with or without a branch scope) to the user's assignments. A user can hold multiple roles simultaneously. Permissions from all assigned roles are combined. Requires 'users.update' permission.

elements.name: Remove button (per role assignment)
elements.description: Removes a single role assignment from the user. If this was the user's only role, they will have no permissions after removal and will not be able to access most pages. Requires 'users.update' permission.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Fetches the next page of 25 users and appends them to the current list.

before_you_start.text: Requires 'users.create' to invite new users.
Requires 'users.update' to edit profiles or manage role assignments.
Requires 'users.deactivate' to deactivate accounts.
Roles must be created first on the Roles page before they can be assigned to users.
Branch-scoped role assignments limit what the user can access to only that branch's data.

common_tasks.task: Invite a new team member
steps: Enter the person's email address in the invite form.
Select their initial role.
Click 'Send Invite'. They will receive an email with a sign-up link valid for 48 hours.

common_tasks.task: Restrict a user's access to a single branch
steps: Click 'Roles' on the user.
Remove any existing global role assignments (those without a branch scope).
Select the appropriate role, select the branch, and click 'Assign Role'.

common_tasks.task: Assign an additional role to an existing user
steps: Click 'Roles' on the user.
Select the role to add and optionally a branch.
Click 'Assign Role'.

common_tasks.task: Update a user's notification preferences
steps: Find the user and click 'Edit'.
Set the WhatsApp locale if needed.
Toggle notification event checkboxes to enable or disable specific event types.
Click 'Save'.

common_tasks.task: Deactivate a user who has left
steps: Find the user in the list.
Click 'Deactivate'. Their access is revoked immediately.
Their historical records (sales, audit logs) are preserved.

warnings: Deactivating a user is immediate — they lose login access the moment you confirm. Reactivate via Edit → Status = ACTIVE.
Removing a user's only role assignment leaves them with no permissions — they will see a restricted view on next login.
Invitation links expire after 48 hours. If a user did not receive or act on the invite, send a new one.

entries.title: Shifts
entries.overview: Open and close POS cash shifts for each branch. When shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, a shift must be open at a branch before staff can process sales there. At shift close, the system records the expected cash based on opening cash plus all cash sales, then compares it to the physical count — any variance is logged and large variances can trigger an approval request for a manager to review.

elements.name: Shift records KPI
elements.description: Total shift records loaded across all branches you have access to, including both open and closed shifts.

elements.name: Open KPI
elements.description: Count of shifts currently open. If this is higher than the number of active branches, an old shift was likely never closed — locate and close it to avoid reconciliation issues.

elements.name: Closed KPI
elements.description: Count of closed shifts in the loaded record set. Use this with a date filter to review daily reconciliation across branches.

elements.name: Branches KPI
elements.description: Number of distinct branches represented in the loaded shift records. If you are reviewing activity across all branches, this confirms how many locations have shift data in the current date range.

elements.name: Branch selector (Open Shift form)
elements.description: Choose which branch you are opening the shift for. Only branches your role has shift access to appear here.

elements.name: Opening cash input
elements.description: Enter the total cash in the till drawer at the start of the shift. This figure is the baseline for calculating the expected closing cash — sales cash is added to it automatically.

elements.name: Notes input (Open Shift)
elements.description: Optional free-text notes about the shift opening. Use this to record any float discrepancies, handover notes, or special circumstances at shift start.

elements.name: Open Shift button
elements.description: Creates and opens the shift for the selected branch. POS sales can now be completed at that branch. If a shift is already open at the same branch, this button is blocked — you must close the existing shift first.

elements.name: Shift selector (Close Shift form)
elements.description: Lists only shifts that are currently open. Select the shift you want to close — typically the open shift at your branch.

elements.name: Closing cash input
elements.description: Enter the physical cash you counted in the till at shift end. The system automatically calculates the expected closing cash (opening cash + all cash sales) and computes the variance when you submit.

elements.name: Variance reason input
elements.description: If closing cash differs from the expected amount, explain why — for example 'Counted short due to change given from manager's float'. A reason is required when variance exceeds the configured threshold in Business Settings.

elements.name: Close Shift button
elements.description: Closes the selected shift and records the variance. If the variance exceeds the approval threshold, an approval request is generated automatically for a manager to review — the POS is not blocked while the request is pending.

elements.name: Shift history list
elements.description: Shows past shifts with branch name, opened and closed timestamps, opening cash, closing cash, and variance amount. Use this for daily reconciliation review or audit purposes.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads additional shift records beyond the initial page.

before_you_start.text: You need the shifts.open permission to open a shift and shifts.close to close one. These are separate permissions — a cashier may have open but not close.
A branch must exist before a shift can be opened for it. Create branches on the Branch Settings page.
Only one shift can be open per branch at a time. If a previous shift was not closed, you must close it before opening a new one.

common_tasks.task: Open a shift at the start of the day
steps: Select your branch from the Branch selector.
Count the opening cash in the till and enter the amount in Opening cash.
Click Open Shift. POS is now enabled at this branch.

common_tasks.task: Close a shift at end of day
steps: Count the physical cash remaining in the till.
Select the open shift from the Shift selector.
Enter the counted amount in Closing cash. If it differs from the expected amount, enter a reason in Variance reason.
Click Close Shift. The shift is closed and any approval workflow triggers automatically.

warnings: Only one shift can be open per branch at a time. If a previous shift was never closed, the Open Shift button is blocked for that branch — close the old shift first.
If shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, POS is blocked for a branch until a shift is open. Staff will see an error at checkout until someone opens the shift.

Large cash variances at close may trigger an approval request. The POS at that branch remains usable while the approval is pending — but the variance is flagged for manager review in the Approvals page.

entries.title: Create Business Account
entries.overview: Use this page to register your business on the system. Fill in your business name, your own name, your email, a password, and choose a subscription plan. After submitting, you will be sent a verification email — you must verify it before you can log in.

before_you_start.text: You need a valid email address that you can access — a verification link will be sent there.
If you were invited by an existing business, do not use this page. Use the Accept Invite page instead.

elements.name: Business Name field
elements.description: The official name of your business. This is what will appear across the system and on receipts.

elements.name: Owner Name field
elements.description: Your full name as the business owner. This becomes your display name in the system.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Your email address. This is your login username and where verification and notification emails will be sent.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Choose a strong password. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below the field (length, complexity).

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Subscription Plan dropdown
elements.description: Choose the plan that fits your business: Starter (basic features), Business (full features), or Enterprise (advanced multi-branch). You can change this later.
elements.notes: This is a trial — you will not be charged immediately.

elements.name: Create Account button
elements.description: Submits your details and creates the business account. You will be redirected to the email verification page.

elements.name: KPI preview cards
elements.description: The three cards at the top (Business Name, Owner, Status) update live as you fill in the form to confirm what you have set.

common_tasks.task: Register a new business
steps: Fill in your Business Name, Owner Name, and Email.
Set a password that meets the requirements.
Choose a subscription plan from the dropdown.
Click Create Account.
Check your email for the verification link and follow it.

warnings: You cannot log in until you verify your email address. Check your inbox (and spam folder) after registering.
Each email address can be registered under multiple businesses, but each business needs its own registration.
Your subscription plan choice here is for the free trial only — you can change it later in Settings.

related_pages.reason: After registering, you are redirected here to verify your email.
After verification, log in here.

entries.title: Stock On Hand
entries.overview: Monitor current inventory levels across branches. Each snapshot row shows how much of a variant is on hand and in transit at a specific branch. You can also define reorder points here to get automatic restocking suggestions.

elements.name: KPI – Snapshots
elements.description: Total branch-variant pairs with stock data matching the current filters. Filter by Branch to see how many SKUs are tracked at one location, or leave unfiltered to see total coverage across all branches.

elements.name: KPI – Reorder Points
elements.description: Number of active reorder-point rules defined for the current branch scope. Each rule monitors one variant at one branch and triggers a Reorder Suggestion when stock falls below the configured minimum.

elements.name: KPI – Suggestions
elements.description: Number of variants currently below their minimum reorder threshold — these need restocking.

elements.name: KPI – Active Branch
elements.description: The branch currently selected in the top-bar branch selector. Stock snapshots on this page are scoped to this branch by default. Use the Branch Filter below to view a different branch without changing the global selector.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Find stock entries by typing part of the variant name or parent product name. Useful for a quick single-item stock check without setting multiple filters.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Restricts snapshot results to a single branch. Always set this when checking stock for a specific location — leaving it unset combines all branches, which can be misleading for restocking decisions.

elements.name: Variant Filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to one specific variant. Combine with Branch Filter to see exactly how much of one SKU is on hand at one location.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by the variant's catalogue status: ACTIVE (currently sold), INACTIVE (suspended), or ARCHIVED (discontinued). In day-to-day stock checks, leave this set to ACTIVE so discontinued items do not clutter the view.

elements.name: Category Filter
elements.description: Restricts results to variants belonging to a specific product category. Useful for doing a category-level stock review — for example, checking all 'Dairy' variants across branches in one view.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid and table view for the snapshots list.

elements.name: Snapshots List
elements.description: Shows each branch + variant combination with on-hand quantity, in-transit quantity, and total.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate pages of snapshot records and adjust page size.

elements.name: Reorder Points – Branch Selector
elements.description: Selects the branch this reorder rule applies to. Reorder points are branch-specific — the same variant can have different minimum thresholds at different locations to reflect varying sales velocity.

elements.name: Reorder Points – Variant Selector
elements.description: Selects the variant this reorder rule will monitor. Once saved, the system compares this variant's on-hand quantity at the chosen branch against the Min Quantity threshold after every stock movement and flags it in Reorder Suggestions.

elements.name: Min Quantity Input
elements.description: The stock level threshold for this variant at the chosen branch. When on-hand quantity drops below this number, the variant is added to the Reorder Suggestions table as a signal that restocking is needed.

elements.name: Reorder Quantity Input
elements.description: The suggested order quantity to restore stock when the minimum threshold is triggered. This value appears in the Reorder Suggestions table as a guide — it does not automatically create a purchase order.

elements.name: Save Reorder Point Button
elements.description: Saves the reorder rule for the selected branch + variant pair. Requires stock.write.

elements.name: Reorder Points Table
elements.description: Lists all active reorder rules with their branch, variant, minimum, and reorder quantities.

elements.name: Reorder Suggestions Table
elements.description: Lists all variants where on-hand quantity has fallen below the minimum threshold, with a suggested reorder quantity.

before_you_start.text: Variants and products must exist before any stock snapshots will appear.
stock.read is required to view snapshots and reorder data; stock.write is required to save reorder points.
Set the correct branch in the top bar or use the branch filter — stock data is branch-specific.

common_tasks.task: Check how much stock is on hand for a variant
steps: Use the Variant Filter to select the variant.
Use the Branch Filter to select the branch.
Read the On Hand quantity from the snapshot card or table row.

common_tasks.task: Set a reorder point to trigger restocking alerts
steps: In the Reorder Points section, select a branch and a variant.
Enter the Min Quantity (the threshold) and Reorder Quantity (how much to order).
Click Save Reorder Point.
Confirm the rule appears in the Reorder Points table below.

warnings: Reorder suggestions are read-only indicators — they do not automatically create purchase orders.
Saving a reorder point with the wrong branch or variant will silently set incorrect thresholds.
In-transit quantity includes stock from approved purchase orders that have not yet been received.
Stock counts and adjustments are done on separate pages — quantities on this page are read-only.

related_pages.reason: Variants must exist for stock snapshots to appear.
View the full history of stock changes for any variant.
Correct stock quantities when they don't match physical inventory.
Reconcile physical inventory with system quantities.

entries.title: Stock Adjustments
entries.overview: Make controlled corrections to stock quantities. Positive adjustments add stock (e.g. found items); negative adjustments remove stock and require a loss reason (damage, theft, expiry). Works in offline mode — adjustments are queued locally and synced when reconnected.

elements.name: KPI – Recent Adjustments
elements.description: Count of adjustment records visible in the history list below. After submitting an adjustment, this count should increase by one immediately. If it does not change, check for an error message — the submission may have failed.

elements.name: KPI – Catalog Variants
elements.description: Total number of variants available for adjustment. If this is zero, no variants exist in the catalogue yet — create products and variants first before attempting stock adjustments.

elements.name: KPI – Open Branches
elements.description: Number of branches available in the Branch Selector for this page. If your branch is missing from the selector, you may not have access to it or it may not yet have been created in Settings → Branches.

elements.name: KPI – Batch Tracking
elements.description: Shows whether batch codes are available for the currently selected variant. When a variant is selected, this updates to indicate if the Batch Selector will show options. If no batches exist yet, create one in the Create Batch section below.

elements.name: Offline PIN Panel
elements.description: Appears when you are offline and a PIN is configured. Enter your PIN to unlock offline adjustment submission.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Adjustment form)
elements.description: Required. The branch where the physical stock change happened. Getting this right is critical — an adjustment to the wrong branch will misstate stock at two locations simultaneously and is difficult to reverse cleanly.

elements.name: Variant Selector (Adjustment form)
elements.description: Required. The specific product variant (SKU) being adjusted. Selecting a product name alone is not sufficient — you must select the exact variant (e.g. 'Soda 500ml', not just 'Soda') to ensure stock moves at the right SKU level.

elements.name: Quantity Input
elements.description: Required. The number of units to add or remove. Enter a positive number — the direction (add or remove) is set by the Type selector. Do not enter a negative value here.

elements.name: Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit of measure for the quantity entered. Must match how the variant is tracked in the catalogue. If the variant is tracked in 'Each' but you are counting by 'Box', convert before entering.

elements.name: Type Selector (Positive / Negative)
elements.description: POSITIVE adds stock; NEGATIVE removes stock and requires selecting a loss reason.

elements.name: Batch Selector
elements.description: Optional. Links the adjustment to a specific batch code for expiry or lot tracking.

elements.name: Loss Reason Selector
elements.description: Required when Type is NEGATIVE. Options: Damaged, Lost, Stolen, Expired, Shrinkage, Other.

elements.name: Reason Notes Input
elements.description: Optional free text to provide context for this adjustment (e.g. 'Recount after delivery — 3 extra cases found on shelf'). Stored in the movement record and visible in audit logs — good notes make investigations significantly easier.

elements.name: Submit Adjustment Button
elements.description: Posts the adjustment. In offline mode, queues it locally for sync when back online.

elements.name: Create Batch – Branch Selector
elements.description: The branch where this batch of goods is physically located. Required before the batch record can be registered — a batch without a branch is invalid.

elements.name: Create Batch – Variant Selector
elements.description: The variant (SKU) this batch code belongs to. Batches are variant-specific — the same lot code from a different product must be registered as a separate batch.

elements.name: Batch Code Input
elements.description: The lot number, batch identifier, or expiry code from the supplier's documentation (e.g. 'LOT-2026-03-A'). Must be unique per variant. Used to trace specific batches in stock movements, adjustments, and counts for lot-level accountability.

elements.name: Expiry Date Input
elements.description: The expiry or use-by date for this batch, if applicable. Once entered, the system can flag near-expiry batches and help prioritise FIFO stock rotation. Leave blank if the product does not have an expiry date.

elements.name: Create Batch Button
elements.description: Registers the batch code so it becomes available in the Batch Selector on the adjustment form. After creating a batch, link adjustments and counts to it for full lot-level traceability. Requires stock.write permission.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between table and card view for the adjustment history list.

elements.name: History Filters
elements.description: Filter the adjustment history list by branch, adjustment type (POSITIVE or NEGATIVE), keyword in the reason notes, and date range. Combine filters to investigate a specific category of stock changes — for example, all NEGATIVE adjustments with reason 'Damaged' this month.

elements.name: Adjustment History List
elements.description: Shows recent POSITIVE and NEGATIVE stock movements with variant, quantity, unit, branch, reason, and timestamp.

before_you_start.text: stock.write is required for all adjustment and batch operations.
Variants and branches must exist before you can make an adjustment.
If working offline and a PIN is required, have your PIN ready before opening this page.

common_tasks.task: Add found stock (positive adjustment)
steps: Select the branch and variant.
Enter the quantity.
Set Type to POSITIVE.
Optionally enter a reason note.
Click Submit Adjustment.

common_tasks.task: Record damaged or lost stock (negative adjustment)
steps: Select the branch and variant.
Enter the quantity to remove.
Set Type to NEGATIVE.
Select the Loss Reason (e.g. Damaged).
Click Submit Adjustment.

warnings: Negative adjustments require a loss reason — the form will not submit without one.
In offline mode, adjustments are queued in IndexedDB and only synced when back online. Do not close the browser tab during offline queuing.
If sync is blocked (a flag shown in the app), offline actions cannot be queued until the block is cleared by an admin.
Batch tracking requires both branch and variant to be selected before batches will load.

related_pages.reason: View the resulting on-hand levels after making adjustments.
Adjustments appear as ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE or ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE movement records.

entries.title: Stock Counts
entries.overview: Reconcile physical inventory with system quantities. Enter how many units you physically counted, and the system records the variance as a STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement. Use the Count Wizard for multi-item sessions.

elements.name: KPI – Recent Counts
elements.description: Number of stock count variance records in the history list. After submitting a count, this should increase by one. If it does not, the submission may have failed — check for an error message before counting again.

elements.name: KPI – Variants
elements.description: Total variants available to count at the selected branch. If this is lower than expected, check that the relevant variants are ACTIVE and marked available for the correct branch.

elements.name: KPI – Branches
elements.description: Number of branches available in the Branch Selector. If your target branch is missing, you may not have access to it or it may not yet be configured in Settings → Branches.

elements.name: KPI – Pending Form
elements.description: Shows 'Ready' when a variant is selected in the form, or 'Waiting' when the form is empty.

elements.name: Open Wizard Link
elements.description: Navigates to the Stock Count Wizard, which lets you count multiple variants in a single guided session. Use the wizard for scheduled stock takes; use this single-item form for quick spot-checks.

elements.name: Branch Selector
elements.description: Required. The branch where the physical count took place. The Expected Quantity shown is specific to this branch — selecting the wrong branch compares your count against the wrong stock level and creates a false variance.

elements.name: Variant Selector
elements.description: Required. The specific variant you physically counted. Selecting a product name is not sufficient — choose the exact variant (e.g. 'Rice 2kg', not just 'Rice') to ensure the variance is recorded at the right SKU.

elements.name: Counted Quantity Input
elements.description: The number of units you physically counted. Enter what you actually found — the system calculates the variance automatically by comparing this value against the Expected Quantity. Double-check before submitting; counts are irreversible.

elements.name: Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit of measure for your counted quantity. Must match the unit the variant is tracked in. If the variant is tracked in 'Each' but you are counting by 'Box', convert before entering to avoid a large false variance.

elements.name: Expected Quantity (read-only)
elements.description: System's current on-hand quantity for this variant at this branch. Used to calculate the variance.

elements.name: Batch Selector
elements.description: Optional. Links this count to a specific batch code. Use this when counting a particular lot — for example, during an expiry date audit or when verifying a specific supplier delivery.

elements.name: Reason Notes Input
elements.description: Optional explanation for this count entry (e.g. 'End of day count', 'Post-delivery recount', 'Spot check — aisle 3'). Stored in the movement record and visible in audit logs — even brief notes make investigations much easier weeks later.

elements.name: Submit Count Button
elements.description: Posts the count. The system calculates variance and creates a STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement record. Requires stock.write.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card and table view for the count history.

elements.name: Count History List
elements.description: Shows recent STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movements with variant, quantity, branch, reason, and timestamp.

before_you_start.text: stock.write is required to submit counts.
The Expected Quantity field shows the system's current value — this is what your counted quantity will be compared against.
For large multi-item counting sessions, use the Count Wizard at /stock/counts/wizard instead of this single-item form.

common_tasks.task: Submit a single-item physical count
steps: Select the branch and variant.
Note the Expected Quantity shown in the read-only field.
Enter your physical Counted Quantity.
Optionally select a batch and add a reason note.
Click Submit Count and confirm success.

warnings: Stock counts are not available in offline mode — you must be online to submit.
Submitting a count creates an irreversible STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement. Double-check your counted quantity before submitting.
If the counted and expected quantities match, no net stock change occurs — but the event is still recorded for audit purposes.

related_pages.reason: Check on-hand levels before and after a count.
Count variance events appear as STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement records.
Use the wizard for guided multi-item count sessions.

entries.title: Stock Count Wizard
entries.overview: Submit physical stock count results for one or more product variants. Each count line records how many units you physically found for a variant at a branch. The system compares your count against the recorded stock level and creates a stock adjustment for any variance — adding stock if you found more, removing it if you found less. Large variances may trigger an approval request before the adjustment is applied.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your position across the two steps: Counts → Review. Add all count lines on the Counts step, then review the full summary before submitting.

elements.name: Branch selector (per line)
elements.description: Choose which branch this count line is for. Each line is branch-specific. If you are counting across multiple branches in one session, add separate lines for each branch — the wizard supports mixed-branch submissions.

elements.name: Variant selector (per line)
elements.description: Select the product variant you physically counted. Once selected, the System quantity field loads the current recorded stock level for that variant at the selected branch so you can compare against your physical count.

elements.name: Counted quantity input
elements.description: Enter the number of units you physically counted for this variant at this branch. This is your actual physical count — enter what you found, not what the system shows. The system calculates the variance automatically.

elements.name: Unit selector (per line)
elements.description: The unit of measure for the counted quantity. Select the same unit the variant is tracked in — usually the base unit (e.g. Case or Piece).

elements.name: Reason input (per line)
elements.description: Explain why you are submitting this count — for example 'Annual stock take', 'Post-delivery count', 'Shrinkage investigation', or 'Spot check'. The reason is stored on the resulting stock adjustment and is visible in the audit log and stock movement history.

elements.name: Batch selector (per line)
elements.description: If batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings, optionally specify which batch you are counting. Leave blank to count all stock of this variant at this branch regardless of batch.

elements.name: System quantity display
elements.description: Shows the current recorded stock level for this variant at this branch for comparison. The difference between this number and your counted quantity is the variance. A positive variance means you found more than recorded; negative means you found fewer.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds another count line. Add one line per variant you are counting. You can count multiple variants and multiple branches in a single wizard session.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that count line from the session. Use this to correct a mistake before advancing to the Review step.

elements.name: Next button
elements.description: Validates all count lines and advances to the Review step. Any lines with missing required fields will be highlighted for correction.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary table showing each variant, branch, system quantity, your counted quantity, and the calculated variance. Review every line carefully — variances with a positive number will add stock; negative will remove it. Once you submit, all adjustments are applied immediately.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Creates a stock adjustment record for every line with a non-zero variance. Each adjustment updates the branch stock level immediately and appears in the stock movement history and audit log. Lines where your count matches the system quantity exactly create no adjustment.

before_you_start.text: You need the stock.write permission.
The branches and variants you are counting must already exist in the system.
If batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings, you can optionally specify a batch for each count line.

common_tasks.task: Submit a stock count for multiple products
steps: Click Add Line for each variant you have physically counted.
For each line, select the branch, variant, enter the counted quantity, choose the unit, and enter a reason.
Click Next to advance to the Review step.
Review the variance column for each line — positive means found more than recorded, negative means fewer.
Click Submit. Stock adjustments are applied immediately for all lines with a variance.

common_tasks.task: Submit a count that reveals shrinkage (missing stock)
steps: Add a count line for the affected variant and branch.
Enter the quantity you physically found (which is less than the system shows).
In Reason, note 'Shrinkage investigation' or describe what may have happened.
Click Next, review the negative variance, then Submit. The stock level is reduced by the variance amount.

common_tasks.task: Count stock across multiple branches in one session
steps: Add one line per variant per branch combination — for example, add a line for 'Warehouse' and a separate line for 'Shop Front' for the same variant.
Fill in all details for each line.
Review the summary on the Review step, then Submit to apply all adjustments at once.

warnings: Each line with a variance creates an immediate stock adjustment — this affects stock levels, reports, and valuation right away. Review the Review step carefully before submitting.
Large variances may trigger approval requests depending on thresholds configured in Business Settings. The adjustment is held until the approval is granted.
You cannot undo a submitted count. If a line was submitted with an incorrect quantity, correct it with another stock count or a manual stock adjustment.

entries.title: Stock Movements
entries.overview: Read-only audit log of every stock change. Each movement has a type (PURCHASE_IN, SALE_OUT, ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE, TRANSFER_IN, etc.), a quantity, the branch, variant, and the user who triggered it. Use filters and date ranges to investigate specific changes.

elements.name: KPI – Movement Rows
elements.description: Number of movement records loaded in the current view. Without filters, the 50 most recent records are shown. Add date, branch, or type filters to narrow to the specific period or event you are investigating.

elements.name: KPI – Actor Options
elements.description: Number of user accounts available in the Actor filter dropdown. This represents the number of staff who have triggered stock movements in the system. If the user you are looking for is missing, they may have been deactivated.

elements.name: KPI – View Mode
elements.description: Shows the current display layout — Table (dense, good for scanning many records quickly) or Cards (more detail per record, good for reading individual movements). Switch using the View Toggle below.

elements.name: KPI – More Pages
elements.description: Indicates whether additional pages of movements exist beyond the current loaded batch.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Filter movements by typing part of the variant's name. Results update as you type. Combine with Branch and Type filters to quickly isolate the movements you are investigating without scrolling through the full history.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Restricts results to stock movements at a specific branch. Always set this when investigating a branch-specific discrepancy — leaving it unset shows all branches mixed together, making individual-branch analysis unreliable.

elements.name: Movement Type Filter
elements.description: Filter by type: OPENING_BALANCE, PURCHASE_IN, SALE_OUT, ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE, ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE, TRANSFER_IN, TRANSFER_OUT, RETURN_IN, RETURN_OUT, or STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE.

elements.name: Actor Filter
elements.description: Shows only movements triggered by a specific user. Use this when reviewing a staff member's stock activity or investigating who made a particular change. Combine with a date range and Type filter for the most targeted results.

elements.name: From Date
elements.description: The start of the date range for movement records. Always set a date range for investigations — without filters, only the 50 most recent records load, which may not include the event you are looking for.

elements.name: To Date
elements.description: The end of the date range. Movements on this date are included. Set both From and To together to isolate a specific period, such as a single shift or a delivery window.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between table (default) and card layout.

elements.name: Movements List
elements.description: Shows each movement with date, branch, variant image, type, quantity, batch code (if any), and the user who triggered it.

elements.name: Load More Button
elements.description: Appends the next batch of movements to the list without resetting what is already visible.

before_you_start.text: Stock movements are created automatically by system operations — you cannot manually create or delete them here.
stock.read (or equivalent) is required to access this page.
Set a date range for investigations — without filters, the most recent 50 records load by default.

common_tasks.task: Investigate why stock dropped unexpectedly
steps: Set the Branch and Variant filters.
Set the Type filter to SALE_OUT or ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE.
Set a date range covering the suspicious period.
Review the Actor column to see who made each change.

common_tasks.task: Confirm a purchase was received into stock
steps: Set the Branch filter and Movement Type to PURCHASE_IN.
Search for the variant name.
Confirm a PURCHASE_IN movement exists with the expected quantity and date.

warnings: Movements are immutable — they cannot be edited or deleted. If a movement is incorrect, create a corrective adjustment on the Adjustments page.
Load More appends records to the list — scrolling back up will still show older results from the same session.
Without a date range, very large datasets may take longer to load.

related_pages.reason: Check on-hand levels before drilling into movement history.
Create a corrective adjustment if you find an incorrect movement.

entries.title: Supplier Returns
entries.overview: Record the return of goods to a supplier. Each return deducts stock from the branch immediately on submission. Returns can be linked to the original purchase or purchase order for a clean audit trail. If the return total exceeds a configured approval threshold, the stock is still deducted but the return is flagged as PENDING until an approver acts.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filter the return list by keyword — searches across return reasons, supplier names, and variant names.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by return status. PENDING means the return was submitted but is awaiting approval (if an approval threshold applies). COMPLETED means the return is fully processed and stock has been deducted. REJECTED means the return approval was denied — check whether a stock adjustment is needed to reverse the deduction.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts the return list to records initiated at a specific branch. Use this when reviewing returns for one location — important if multiple branches deal with the same supplier and you want branch-level clarity.

elements.name: Supplier filter
elements.description: Restrict the return list to returns sent to a specific supplier. Useful for reviewing your full returns history with a particular supplier.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Filters the return list to records created within the selected window. Use this to reconcile supplier returns for a specific period, such as a monthly supplier review or a quarterly credit check.

elements.name: Branch selector (create form)
elements.description: The branch from which the goods are being returned. Stock is deducted from this branch immediately when the return is submitted.

elements.name: Supplier selector
elements.description: The supplier you are returning goods to. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear. The return is associated with this supplier for reporting and audit purposes.

elements.name: Purchase selector
elements.description: Optional. Link the return to a specific purchase record. Linking creates a direct audit trail between the original purchase and the return — recommended when the connection is clear.

elements.name: Purchase Order selector
elements.description: Optional. Link the return to a specific purchase order. If the goods were received via a PO, link to that PO here for a complete trace from order to return.

elements.name: Reason input
elements.description: Required. Explain why the goods are being returned — for example 'Items arrived with damaged packaging', 'Wrong SKU delivered', or 'Quantity over-delivered'. This reason is stored in the audit log and is visible to approvers if approval is required.

elements.name: Return line row
elements.description: One row per item being returned. For each line, select the variant, enter the quantity being returned, the unit cost, and the receiving line the goods originally came from. Each line deducts from the branch's stock when submitted.

elements.name: Receiving line selector (per row)
elements.description: Links the returned stock back to the specific receiving session it came from. Required — stock can only be returned if it was received first through this system. The selector shows only receiving lines for the chosen variant at the chosen branch.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds another return line for an additional variant. Use this when returning multiple different items to the supplier in the same return record.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Creates the return record and deducts stock from the branch immediately. Cannot be undone — verify all quantities, variants, and receiving line selections before clicking. If an approval is triggered, the return shows as PENDING but stock is already deducted.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the return total exceeds the approval threshold set in Business Settings. Stock is deducted immediately on submission, but the return record is marked PENDING until approved on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next page of return records to the current list. The page does not automatically load all records — click this when you need to review returns beyond the initial batch.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
A receiving record must exist for the items being returned — you can only return stock that was received through this system. The Receiving line selector shows only records that match.

common_tasks.task: Return damaged goods to a supplier
steps: Fill in Branch and Supplier. In Reason, describe the issue clearly — for example 'Items arrived damaged, packaging crushed on delivery'.
Click Add Line and select the variant being returned.
Select the receiving line the goods came from (the receiving line selector shows only matching records).
Enter the quantity being returned and the unit cost.
Click Submit. Stock is deducted from the branch immediately.

common_tasks.task: Return a wrong item delivered by a supplier
steps: Set Branch and Supplier. In Reason, note 'Wrong SKU delivered — received [variant X] but ordered [variant Y]'.
Add a return line for the incorrect variant, select its receiving line, and enter the quantity and cost.
Click Submit. After the return is processed, raise a new purchase order for the correct item.

common_tasks.task: Check the status of a submitted return
steps: Use the Status filter to show PENDING returns.
If a return is PENDING, it is awaiting approval on the Approvals page — ask a manager to review it.
If a return shows REJECTED, check whether a manual stock adjustment is needed to reverse the deduction.

warnings: Stock is deducted from the branch immediately when you submit — even if the return triggers an approval workflow. If the approval is later rejected, the stock deduction is not reversed automatically — a manual stock adjustment will be needed.

The Receiving line selector only shows lines that match the selected variant at the selected branch. If the expected line is missing, the goods may have been received under a different branch, or the receiving record may not exist yet.
Returns cannot be edited after submission. If you submitted incorrect quantities or the wrong variant, contact your supervisor — a correcting stock adjustment may be required.

entries.title: Suppliers
entries.overview: Manage your supplier directory. Create and maintain vendor profiles, set lead times so the system can suggest delivery ETAs when creating purchase orders, track outstanding balances owed to each supplier, and review recent order activity. Suppliers must exist here before you can create purchase orders or record receiving against them.

elements.name: KPI — Suppliers
elements.description: Total number of supplier records matching the current filters. If no filters are set, this is your full supplier count.

elements.name: KPI — Active
elements.description: Count of suppliers with ACTIVE status. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear in purchase order and receiving selectors — if a supplier is missing from those selectors, check they are not INACTIVE or ARCHIVED here.

elements.name: KPI — Needs attention
elements.description: Count of suppliers that are INACTIVE or ARCHIVED. Review these periodically — suppliers that are no longer trading should be ARCHIVED so they no longer appear in purchase workflows.

elements.name: KPI — Lead-time set
elements.description: Count of suppliers that have a lead time (in days) configured. Lead times are used to calculate a suggested delivery ETA when creating purchase orders. If this count is low, consider setting lead times on your regular suppliers.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter suppliers by name, phone, or email as you type. Use this to quickly locate a supplier when your list is long.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrow the list to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED suppliers. ARCHIVED suppliers are hidden from purchase workflows but their historical records are preserved.

elements.name: Balance due filter
elements.description: Filter to suppliers with an outstanding balance (Yes) or a clear balance (No). Use this to identify which suppliers you owe money to before running payments.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active filters and reloads the full supplier list.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (shows full details per supplier) and table layout (better for scanning many suppliers at once).

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. The supplier's business or trading name. This name appears in purchase order selectors, receiving forms, and reports — use the name you recognise them by.

elements.name: Phone field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's contact phone number. Stored for reference — not used in automated workflows.

elements.name: Email field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's contact email address, stored for reference. Used when generating purchase order notification emails if that feature is enabled. Can be added later by editing the supplier record.

elements.name: Address field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's physical or postal address. Useful for delivery coordination and printed purchase orders.

elements.name: Notes field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Free-text notes about the supplier — for example payment terms, preferred contact method, or product specialities. Visible to all users who can see this supplier.

elements.name: Lead time days field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The typical number of days between placing an order and receiving the goods. When set, the Purchase Order Wizard displays a suggested ETA (today + lead time days) when you select this supplier. Set this accurately for better planning.

elements.name: Create Supplier button
elements.description: Saves the new supplier record. The supplier is immediately available in purchase order and receiving selectors. Requires the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: Edit button (per supplier)
elements.description: Opens an inline edit form for that supplier's name, phone, email, address, notes, lead time, and status. Requires the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: View Activity / Hide Activity toggle
elements.description: Expands or collapses a panel showing the supplier's 5 most recent purchase orders and 5 most recent purchases. Use this for a quick snapshot of your order history with a supplier without leaving the page.

elements.name: Related Notes panel
elements.description: A collapsible notes thread attached to each supplier card. Team members can add timestamped notes visible to everyone — for example, recording a price negotiation outcome or a delivery complaint. Notes are separate from the main Notes field on the profile.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits all edited fields immediately. Cancel discards all changes and closes the edit form without saving.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Fetches the next page of suppliers and appends them to the list. Use this if your search did not return the supplier you expected — they may be on a later page.

before_you_start.text: You need the suppliers.write permission to create or edit supplier records.
Set lead time days accurately — this directly affects the ETA suggestions shown when creating purchase orders.
The Activity panel loads purchase order and purchase data on demand — click View Activity to see it rather than loading it for every supplier at once.

common_tasks.task: Add a new supplier
steps: Fill in the Name (required) and optionally phone, email, address, and notes.
If you know the supplier's typical delivery time, enter it in Lead time days.
Click Create Supplier. The supplier is immediately available in purchase workflows.

common_tasks.task: Update supplier contact details or lead time
steps: Find the supplier card and click Edit.
Update the relevant fields.
Click Save. Changes take effect immediately — the new lead time will be used in the next purchase order for this supplier.

common_tasks.task: View recent orders for a supplier
steps: Find the supplier card.
Click View Activity to expand the recent order panel.
The panel shows the 5 most recent purchase orders and 5 most recent purchases for that supplier.

common_tasks.task: Add a note to a supplier
steps: Expand the Related Notes panel on the supplier card.
Type your note and submit. The note is timestamped and visible to all users who can see this supplier.

common_tasks.task: Find suppliers with outstanding balances
steps: Set Balance due filter to Yes.
The list shows only suppliers you currently owe money to.
Use this before processing supplier payments to know who to pay.

warnings: Setting a supplier to ARCHIVED hides them from purchase order and receiving selectors. Their historical records are preserved, but you cannot create new orders for an archived supplier — set them back to ACTIVE if needed.
Lead time days affects the ETA hint in the Purchase Order Wizard. If set too low, staff may expect deliveries earlier than the supplier can actually deliver — set it accurately.

entries.title: Transfers
entries.overview: View and manage stock transfers between branches. This page shows all transfers in all statuses — use it to see what is in transit to your branch, receive incoming transfers to update your stock, and track the history of past transfers. To create a new transfer, use the Transfer Wizard.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Find transfers by keyword — searches across transfer codes, branch names, and item names. Use this to locate a specific transfer quickly.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Filter by source or destination branch. Set it to your branch as destination to see only incoming transfers, or as source to see outgoing ones.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by transfer status. PENDING — created, stock deducted from source, not yet in transit. IN_TRANSIT — dispatched by source branch, awaiting confirmation at destination. RECEIVED — destination branch confirmed receipt and stock has been added. PENDING_APPROVAL — waiting for manager approval before receipt can be confirmed.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restricts the transfer list to records created within the selected window. Use this when reconciling inter-branch stock movements for a specific period — for example, all transfers made in a particular week or month.

elements.name: Transfer card / row
